name: nextjs
guidance: Prefer Server Components, inspect local Next docs when required, keep client boundaries small.

name: react
guidance: Keep state local, avoid effects for derivations, use composition for reusable UI.

name: typescript
guidance: Encode component contracts, avoid any, use discriminated unions for complex states.

name: tailwind
guidance: Use tokenized utilities, mobile-first breakpoints, and avoid arbitrary values without reason.

name: shadcn
guidance: Inspect components.json, extend variants, preserve Radix accessibility behavior.

name: radix
guidance: Respect focus, roles, portals, and data-state styling.

name: tanstack
guidance: Use for complex table/query state; define empty/loading/error paths.

name: zustand
guidance: Use small stores with selectors; avoid globalizing local state.

name: redux
guidance: Use for broad app state with clear slices; avoid boilerplate for local UI.

name: prisma
guidance: Keep DB access server-side; model loading/error states in UI.

name: drizzle
guidance: Use typed queries server-side and keep schema concepts out of generic UI.

name: rtl
guidance: Use logical properties, dir boundaries, icon mirroring checks, and mixed-LTR isolation.

name: angular: Use standalone components
guidance: Angular 17+ default; no NgModule needed. Do: Standalone components for all new code. Avoid: NgModule-based components for new projects. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components/importing

details.Description: Angular 17+ default; no NgModule needed
details.Do: Standalone components for all new code

name: angular: Use signals for state
guidance: Signals are Angular's reactive primitive for fine-grained reactivity. Do: Signals for component state over class properties. Avoid: Mutable class properties without signals. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/signals

details.Description: Signals are Angular's reactive primitive for fine-grained reactivity
details.Do: Signals for component state over class properties

name: angular: Use @if/@for/@switch control flow
guidance: Built-in control flow syntax replaces *ngIf/*ngFor directives. Do: @if and @for in templates. Avoid: *ngIf and *ngFor structural directives. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/templates/control-flow

details.Description: Built-in control flow syntax replaces *ngIf/*ngFor directives

name: angular: Use input() and output() signals
guidance: Signal-based inputs/outputs replace @Input()/@Output() decorators. Do: input() and output() for component API. Avoid: @Input() and @Output() decorators. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components/inputs

details.Description: Signal-based inputs/outputs replace @Input()/@Output() decorators
details.Do: input() and output() for component API

name: angular: Use content projection
guidance: ng-content for flexible component composition. Do: ng-content with select for named slots. Avoid: Rigid templates that can't be customized. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components/content-projection

details.Description: ng-content for flexible component composition
details.Do: ng-content with select for named slots
details.Don't: Rigid templates that can't be customized

name: angular: Keep components small
guidance: Single responsibility; components should do one thing. Do: Extract sub-components when template exceeds 50 lines. Avoid: Monolithic components handling multiple concerns. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components

details.Description: Single responsibility; components should do one thing
details.Do: Extract sub-components when template exceeds 50 lines

name: angular: Use OnPush change detection
guidance: Reduces re-renders by only checking on input changes or signal updates. Do: OnPush for all components. Avoid: Default change detection strategy. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components/lifecycle

details.Description: Reduces re-renders by only checking on input changes or signal updates

name: angular: Avoid direct DOM manipulation
guidance: Use renderer or ElementRef sparingly; prefer template bindings. Do: Template bindings and Angular directives. Avoid: Direct document.querySelector or innerHTML. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/components/host-elements

details.Description: Use renderer or ElementRef sparingly; prefer template bindings

name: angular: Lazy load feature routes
guidance: Load route chunks on demand to reduce initial bundle. Do: loadComponent() for all feature routes. Avoid: Eager-loaded routes in app config. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/routing/lazy-loading

details.Description: Load route chunks on demand to reduce initial bundle

name: angular: Use route guards with functional API
guidance: Protect routes with canActivate/canMatch functional guards. Do: Functional guards returning boolean or UrlTree. Avoid: Class-based guards with CanActivate interface. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/routing/common-router-tasks#preventing-unauthorized-access

details.Guideline: Use route guards with functional API
details.Description: Protect routes with canActivate/canMatch functional guards
details.Do: Functional guards returning boolean or UrlTree

name: angular: Use route resolvers for data
guidance: Pre-fetch data before route activation using resolve. Do: ResolveFn for route data. Avoid: Fetching data in ngOnInit causing flash of empty state. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/routing/common-router-tasks#resolve

details.Description: Pre-fetch data before route activation using resolve
details.Don't: Fetching data in ngOnInit causing flash of empty state
details.Code Bad: Fetch in ngOnInit with loading state flickering

name: angular: Type route params with inject
guidance: Use inject(ActivatedRoute) with signals or toSignal. Do: Typed route params via ActivatedRoute. Avoid: Untyped route.snapshot.params string access. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/api/router/ActivatedRoute

details.Description: Use inject(ActivatedRoute) with signals or toSignal

name: angular: Use nested routes for layouts
guidance: Compose shared layouts using router-outlet nesting. Do: Nested routes with shared layout components. Avoid: Duplicating layout code across routes. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/routing/router-tutorial-toh#child-route-configuration

details.Description: Compose shared layouts using router-outlet nesting
details.Do: Nested routes with shared layout components
details.Code Bad: Duplicate header/sidebar in each route component

name: angular: Configure preloading strategies
guidance: Preload lazy modules in background after initial load. Do: PreloadAllModules or custom strategy. Avoid: No preloading causing delayed navigation. Severity: Low. Docs: https://angular.dev/api/router/PreloadAllModules

details.Description: Preload lazy modules in background after initial load

name: angular: Use signals for local state
guidance: Signals provide synchronous reactive state without RxJS overhead. Do: signal() for component-local reactive state. Avoid: BehaviorSubject for simple local state. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/signals

details.Description: Signals provide synchronous reactive state without RxJS overhead
details.Code Good: const items = signal<Item[]>([]); addItem(i: Item) { this.items.update(arr => [...arr i]) }

name: angular: Use computed() for derived state
guidance: Lazily evaluated derived values that update when dependencies change. Do: computed() for values derived from other signals. Avoid: Duplicated state or manual sync. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/signals#computed-signals

details.Description: Lazily evaluated derived values that update when dependencies change
details.Do: computed() for values derived from other signals
details.Code Good: readonly total = computed(() => this.items().reduce((s i) => s + i.price 0))

name: angular: Use effect() carefully
guidance: Effects run side effects when signals change; avoid overuse. Do: effect() for side effects like logging or localStorage sync. Avoid: effect() for deriving state (use computed instead). Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/signals#effects

details.Description: Effects run side effects when signals change; avoid overuse
details.Do: effect() for side effects like logging or localStorage sync
details.Don't: effect() for deriving state (use computed instead)

name: angular: Use NgRx Signal Store for complex state
guidance: NgRx Signal Store is the modern lightweight state management for Angular. Do: @ngrx/signals SignalStore for feature state. Avoid: Full NgRx reducer/action/effect boilerplate for simple state. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://ngrx.io/guide/signals

details.Guideline: Use NgRx Signal Store for complex state
details.Description: NgRx Signal Store is the modern lightweight state management for Angular
details.Don't: Full NgRx reducer/action/effect boilerplate for simple state
details.Code Good: const Store = signalStore(withState({ count: 0 }) withMethods(s => ({ increment: () => patchState(s { count: s.count() + 1 }) })))

name: angular: Inject services for shared state
guidance: Services with signals share state across components without a store. Do: Injectable service with signals for cross-component state. Avoid: Prop drilling or @Input chains for shared state. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/di/creating-injectable-service

details.Description: Services with signals share state across components without a store
details.Do: Injectable service with signals for cross-component state
details.Don't: Prop drilling or @Input chains for shared state

name: angular: Avoid mixing RxJS and signals unnecessarily
guidance: Use toSignal() to bridge RxJS into signal world at the boundary. Do: toSignal() to convert observable to signal at component edge. Avoid: Subscribing in components and storing in signal manually. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/rxjs-interop

details.Guideline: Avoid mixing RxJS and signals unnecessarily
details.Description: Use toSignal() to bridge RxJS into signal world at the boundary
details.Do: toSignal() to convert observable to signal at component edge
details.Don't: Subscribing in components and storing in signal manually

name: angular: Use typed reactive forms
guidance: FormGroup/FormControl with explicit generics for compile-time safety. Do: FormBuilder with typed controls. Avoid: Untyped FormControl or any casts. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/forms/typed-forms

details.Description: FormGroup/FormControl with explicit generics for compile-time safety

name: angular: Use reactive forms over template-driven
guidance: Reactive forms scale better and are fully testable. Do: ReactiveFormsModule for all non-trivial forms. Avoid: FormsModule with ngModel for complex forms. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/forms/reactive-forms

details.Description: Reactive forms scale better and are fully testable
details.Don't: FormsModule with ngModel for complex forms

name: angular: Write custom validators as functions
guidance: Functional validators are composable and tree-shakeable. Do: ValidatorFn functions for custom validation. Avoid: Class-based validators implementing Validator interface. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/forms/form-validation#custom-validators

details.Description: Functional validators are composable and tree-shakeable
details.Code Good: const noSpaces: ValidatorFn = ctrl => ctrl.value?.includes(' ') ? { noSpaces: true } : null

name: angular: Use updateOn for performance
guidance: Control when validation runs to avoid per-keystroke validation overhead. Do: updateOn: 'blur' or 'submit' for expensive validators. Avoid: Default updateOn: 'change' for async validators. Severity: Low. Docs: https://angular.dev/api/forms/AbstractControl#updateOn

details.Description: Control when validation runs to avoid per-keystroke validation overhead

name: angular: Use FormArray for dynamic fields
guidance: FormArray manages variable-length lists of controls. Do: FormArray for add/remove field scenarios. Avoid: Manually tracking index-based controls. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/forms/reactive-forms#using-the-formarray-class

details.Description: FormArray manages variable-length lists of controls
details.Code Good: get items(): FormArray { return this.form.get('items') as FormArray }

name: angular: Display validation errors clearly
guidance: Use form control touched and dirty states to show errors at the right time. Do: Show errors after field is touched. Avoid: Show all errors on page load. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/forms/form-validation

details.Description: Use form control touched and dirty states to show errors at the right time
details.Do: Show errors after field is touched
details.Don't: Show all errors on page load

name: angular: Apply OnPush to all components
guidance: OnPush + signals eliminates most unnecessary change detection cycles. Do: OnPush change detection everywhere. Avoid: Default strategy which checks entire tree on every event. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/best-practices/skipping-component-subtrees

details.Description: OnPush + signals eliminates most unnecessary change detection cycles
details.Don't: Default strategy which checks entire tree on every event

name: angular: Use trackBy in @for blocks
guidance:
Stable identity for list items prevents full DOM re-creation on change. Do: track item.id in @for. Avoid: @for (item of items; track item.id) {
  • {{ item.name }}
    • }. Severity: https://angular.dev/guide/templates/control-flow#track-and-identity. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Stable identity for list items prevents full DOM re-creation on change

name: angular: Avoid subscribe in components
guidance: Subscriptions leak and cause bugs; prefer async pipe or toSignal. Do: toSignal() or async pipe instead of manual subscribe. Avoid: Manual subscribe without unsubscribe in ngOnDestroy. Severity: High. Docs: https://angular.dev/guide/rxjs-interop

guidance: Harnesses provide a stable testing API that survives template refactors. Do: MatButtonHarness and custom HarnessLoader. Avoid: Direct native element queries that break on template changes. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://material.angular.io/cdk/test-harnesses/overview

name: avalonia: Use DataGrid for tabular data
guidance: DataGrid is a separate Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid NuGet package and requires its theme StyleInclude in App.axaml. Do: DataGrid after adding package and StyleInclude for the matching theme. Avoid: Custom Grid layouts for tabular data or DataGrid without the theme StyleInclude. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/reference/controls/datagrid/

details.Description: DataGrid is a separate Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid NuGet package and requires its theme StyleInclude in App.axaml
details.Do: DataGrid after adding package and StyleInclude for the matching theme
details.Don't: Custom Grid layouts for tabular data or DataGrid without the theme StyleInclude
details.Code Good: <DataGrid ItemsSource="{Binding Items}" AutoGenerateColumns="False"/> with <StyleInclude Source="avares://Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid/Themes/Fluent.xaml"/> in App.axaml
details.Code Bad: <DataGrid/> with no package reference or no StyleInclude (renders unstyled)

guidance: Avalonia uses TreeDataTemplate for hierarchical data - HierarchicalDataTemplate is WPF only. Do: TreeDataTemplate inside TreeView.ItemTemplate with ItemsSource pointing at child collection. Avoid: HierarchicalDataTemplate copied from WPF or nested ItemsControls. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/reference/controls/treeview-1

details.Description: Avalonia uses TreeDataTemplate for hierarchical data - HierarchicalDataTemplate is WPF only
details.Do: TreeDataTemplate inside TreeView.ItemTemplate with ItemsSource pointing at child collection
details.Don't: HierarchicalDataTemplate copied from WPF or nested ItemsControls

name: avalonia: Use ViewLocator pattern
guidance: Convention-based View-ViewModel resolution. Do: ViewLocator for automatic view resolution. Avoid: Manual view instantiation and DataContext wiring. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/data-templates/view-locator

details.Description: Convention-based View-ViewModel resolution
details.Don't: Manual view instantiation and DataContext wiring
details.Code Good: class ViewLocator : IDataTemplate { Build(object data) => new MainView(); }
details.Code Bad: new MainView { DataContext = new MainViewModel() } everywhere

name: avalonia: Use dependency injection
guidance: Register services in a Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection container during startup before any view is constructed - resolve ViewModels through the provider not via a static ServiceLocator. Do: Build the ServiceProvider in BuildAvaloniaApp or OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted then resolve ViewModels from it. Avoid: Static ServiceLocator or new-ing ViewModels inline in code-behind. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/app-development/dependency-injection

details.Description: Register services in a Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection container during startup before any view is constructed - resolve ViewModels through the provider not via a static ServiceLocator
details.Do: Build the ServiceProvider in BuildAvaloniaApp or OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted then resolve ViewModels from it
details.Don't: Static ServiceLocator or new-ing ViewModels inline in code-behind
details.Code Good: services.AddSingleton<IDataService, DataService>(); services.AddTransient<MainViewModel>(); var provider = services.BuildServiceProvider(); // wired before windows are created
details.Code Bad: ServiceLocator.Current.GetInstance<IDataService>() called from random ViewModels with no registration ordering

guidance: Configure platform features and services at startup. Do: AppBuilder with UsePlatformDetect and fluent API. Avoid: Manual platform initialization. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/fundamentals/application-lifetimes

details.Do: AppBuilder with UsePlatformDetect and fluent API

name: avalonia: Initialize MainWindow in OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted
guidance: Override OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted on App and check ApplicationLifetime - on desktop cast to IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime to set MainWindow and ShutdownMode; never create windows in the App constructor before the framework is ready. Do: Override OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted and pattern-match on IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime for desktop-only setup. Avoid: Creating windows in the App constructor or assuming the same lifetime type on every platform. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/fundamentals/application-lifetimes

details.Description: Override OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted on App and check ApplicationLifetime - on desktop cast to IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime to set MainWindow and ShutdownMode; never create windows in the App constructor before the framework is ready
details.Do: Override OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted and pattern-match on IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime for desktop-only setup
details.Don't: Creating windows in the App constructor or assuming the same lifetime type on every platform
details.Code Good: public override void OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted() { if (ApplicationLifetime is IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime desktop) { desktop.MainWindow = new MainWindow(); desktop.ShutdownMode = ShutdownMode.OnMainWindowClose; } base.OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted(); }
details.Code Bad: public App() { new MainWindow().Show(); } // window created before framework init and ignores lifetime type

name: avalonia: Use CSS-like keyframe animations
guidance: Avalonia supports declarative animations in XAML and code. Do: Animation with KeyFrame and Setter for property animations. Avoid: Manual timer-based property updates. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/graphics-animation/animations

details.Description: Avalonia supports declarative animations in XAML and code
details.Do: Animation with KeyFrame and Setter for property animations
details.Code Bad: DispatcherTimer ticking to update Opacity manually

name: avalonia: Use Transitions for implicit animations
guidance: Automatic animation when property values change. Do: Transitions collection on controls for smooth changes. Avoid: Instant property changes with no visual feedback. Severity: Low. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/graphics-animation/control-transitions

details.Description: Automatic animation when property values change
details.Do: Transitions collection on controls for smooth changes
details.Don't: Instant property changes with no visual feedback
details.Code Bad: Direct property set with no transition

name: avalonia: Use compiled bindings and TrimmerRoots.xml for PublishAot
guidance: Avalonia 11+ supports Native AOT for self-contained desktop deployments; XAML reflection paths must use compiled bindings or be preserved via TrimmerRoots so trimming does not strip them. Do: x:CompileBindings=True on every view plus TrimmerRoots.xml for runtime-resolved types. Avoid: PublishAot with reflection-based {Binding} markup or trimming without checking warnings. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/deployment/native-aot

details.Guideline: Use compiled bindings and TrimmerRoots.xml for PublishAot
details.Description: Avalonia 11+ supports Native AOT for self-contained desktop deployments; XAML reflection paths must use compiled bindings or be preserved via TrimmerRoots so trimming does not strip them
details.Do: x:CompileBindings=True on every view plus TrimmerRoots.xml for runtime-resolved types
details.Don't: PublishAot with reflection-based {Binding} markup or trimming without checking warnings
details.Code Good: <UserControl x:CompileBindings="True" x:DataType="vm:MainViewModel"/> with <PublishAot>true</PublishAot> and TrimmerRoots.xml listing reflected types
details.Code Bad: <PublishAot>true</PublishAot> with default <Binding> markup and no TrimmerRoots configuration

name: avalonia: Marshal cross-thread work to the UI thread
guidance: Avalonia controls and bound properties are not thread-safe and touching them off the UI thread throws InvalidOperationException. Do: Dispatcher.UIThread.Post or InvokeAsync to bounce work back to the UI thread. Avoid: Direct property writes from Task.Run or background threads. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/app-development/threading

details.Guideline: Marshal cross-thread work to the UI thread
details.Description: Avalonia controls and bound properties are not thread-safe and touching them off the UI thread throws InvalidOperationException
details.Do: Dispatcher.UIThread.Post or InvokeAsync to bounce work back to the UI thread
details.Don't: Direct property writes from Task.Run or background threads
details.Code Bad: Task.Run(() => { Status = "Done"; }); // throws Call from invalid thread

name: avalonia: Use AsyncRelayCommand or ReactiveCommand for async work
guidance: Async-aware commands disable themselves while running and surface CancellationToken so users cannot double-invoke a long operation. Do: [RelayCommand] async Task method or ReactiveCommand.CreateFromTask. Avoid: async void event handlers or fire-and-forget Task.Run from a click handler. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/input-interaction/commanding

details.Guideline: Use AsyncRelayCommand or ReactiveCommand for async work
details.Description: Async-aware commands disable themselves while running and surface CancellationToken so users cannot double-invoke a long operation
details.Don't: async void event handlers or fire-and-forget Task.Run from a click handler
details.Code Good: [RelayCommand] private async Task LoadAsync(CancellationToken ct) { await _api.GetAsync(ct); }
details.Code Bad: private async void OnClick(object s, RoutedEventArgs e) { await LongOperation(); }

name: avalonia: Use DynamicResource for theme-aware brushes
guidance: ResourceDictionary.ThemeDictionaries entries must be looked up via DynamicResource - StaticResource resolves once at load and won't update when the active theme variant changes. Do: DynamicResource for brushes and colors that follow the active theme variant. Avoid: Hardcoded hex colors or StaticResource for values that should follow theme. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/app-development/resource-dictionary

details.Description: ResourceDictionary.ThemeDictionaries entries must be looked up via DynamicResource - StaticResource resolves once at load and won't update when the active theme variant changes
details.Do: DynamicResource for brushes and colors that follow the active theme variant
details.Don't: Hardcoded hex colors or StaticResource for values that should follow theme
details.Code Bad: <Border Background="#FF0078D4"/> while the rest of the app honors light/dark variants

name: avalonia: Use HotKey or KeyBinding for keyboard shortcuts
guidance: Built-in HotKey on ICommandSource and Window.KeyBindings handle modifier keys focus scoping and cross-platform Ctrl/Cmd mapping. Do: HotKey on a command-bound control or KeyBinding on the Window. Avoid: Manual KeyDown handlers checking Key and KeyModifiers. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/input-interaction/mouse-and-keyboard-shortcuts

details.Guideline: Use HotKey or KeyBinding for keyboard shortcuts
details.Description: Built-in HotKey on ICommandSource and Window.KeyBindings handle modifier keys focus scoping and cross-platform Ctrl/Cmd mapping
details.Do: HotKey on a command-bound control or KeyBinding on the Window
details.Don't: Manual KeyDown handlers checking Key and KeyModifiers
details.Code Bad: OnKeyDown checking e.Key == Key.S && e.KeyModifiers == KeyModifiers.Control

name: avalonia: Customize window chrome with ExtendClientAreaToDecorationsHint
guidance: Set ExtendClientAreaToDecorationsHint to extend content into the title bar area and tag a region with WindowDecorationProperties.ElementRole=TitleBar to keep native drag and maximize behavior. Do: ExtendClientAreaToDecorationsHint plus a region tagged ElementRole=TitleBar. Avoid: SystemDecorations=None with hand-rolled PointerPressed dragging in code-behind. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/app-development/window-management

details.Description: Set ExtendClientAreaToDecorationsHint to extend content into the title bar area and tag a region with WindowDecorationProperties.ElementRole=TitleBar to keep native drag and maximize behavior
details.Do: ExtendClientAreaToDecorationsHint plus a region tagged ElementRole=TitleBar
details.Don't: SystemDecorations=None with hand-rolled PointerPressed dragging in code-behind
details.Code Bad: <Window SystemDecorations="None"> with manual BeginMoveDrag from code-behind

name: avalonia: Use TopLevel.StorageProvider for file pickers
guidance: The legacy OpenFileDialog/SaveFileDialog APIs are obsolete in Avalonia 11 - use TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this).StorageProvider with OpenFilePickerAsync/SaveFilePickerAsync/OpenFolderPickerAsync which returns IStorageFile/IStorageFolder and works on desktop mobile and browser. Do: TopLevel.StorageProvider with OpenFilePickerAsync and FilePickerOpenOptions. Avoid: OpenFileDialog or SaveFileDialog from older Avalonia samples or copied from WPF. Severity: High. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/concepts/services/storage-provider/

details.Description: The legacy OpenFileDialog/SaveFileDialog APIs are obsolete in Avalonia 11 - use TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this).StorageProvider with OpenFilePickerAsync/SaveFilePickerAsync/OpenFolderPickerAsync which returns IStorageFile/IStorageFolder and works on desktop mobile and browser
details.Don't: OpenFileDialog or SaveFileDialog from older Avalonia samples or copied from WPF
details.Code Good: var top = TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this); var files = await top.StorageProvider.OpenFilePickerAsync(new FilePickerOpenOptions { AllowMultiple = false });
details.Code Bad: var dlg = new OpenFileDialog(); var paths = await dlg.ShowAsync(window); // obsolete API

name: avalonia: Use TrayIcon for system tray icon
guidance: TrayIcon shows a native system tray/notification-area icon with a NativeMenu - declare it via the Application.TrayIcon.Icons attached property in App.axaml; works on Windows macOS and most Linux desktops. Do: TrayIcon with NativeMenu inside TrayIcon.Icons on the Application. Avoid: Custom borderless window pretending to be a tray icon or per-platform native interop. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/controls/tray-icon

details.Guideline: Use TrayIcon for system tray icon
details.Description: TrayIcon shows a native system tray/notification-area icon with a NativeMenu - declare it via the Application.TrayIcon.Icons attached property in App.axaml; works on Windows macOS and most Linux desktops
details.Do: TrayIcon with NativeMenu inside TrayIcon.Icons on the Application
details.Don't: Custom borderless window pretending to be a tray icon or per-platform native interop
details.Code Bad: Hidden Window with custom shell-notification-area P/Invoke

name: avalonia: Use OnPlatform and OnFormFactor markup for per-OS values
guidance: OnPlatform and OnFormFactor markup extensions resolve to a different value per OS or form factor at XAML load time and replace if-statements in code-behind for tweaks like fonts spacing or icon sizes. Do: OnPlatform with Default Windows macOS Linux entries directly in the property setter. Avoid: RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform branches in code-behind to set XAML properties. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://docs.avaloniaui.net/docs/platform-specific-guides/xaml

details.Guideline: Use OnPlatform and OnFormFactor markup for per-OS values
details.Description: OnPlatform and OnFormFactor markup extensions resolve to a different value per OS or form factor at XAML load time and replace if-statements in code-behind for tweaks like fonts spacing or icon sizes
details.Do: OnPlatform with Default Windows macOS Linux entries directly in the property setter
details.Don't: RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform branches in code-behind to set XAML properties
details.Code Good: <TextBlock FontFamily="{OnPlatform Default='Inter', Windows='Segoe UI', macOS='SF Pro Text', Linux='Ubuntu'}"/>
details.Code Bad: if (OperatingSystem.IsWindows()) textBlock.FontFamily = new("Segoe UI"); else if (OperatingSystem.IsMacOS()) ...

details.Description: Start with mobile styles and add breakpoints

name: html-tailwind: Breakpoint testing
guidance: Test at standard breakpoints. Do: 320 375 768 1024 1280 1536. Avoid: Only test on development device. Severity: High. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Test at standard breakpoints

name: html-tailwind: Hidden/shown utilities
guidance: Control visibility per breakpoint. Do: hidden md:block. Avoid: Different content per breakpoint. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/display

details.Description: Control visibility per breakpoint

name: html-tailwind: Button sizing
guidance: Consistent button dimensions. Do: px-4 py-2 or px-6 py-3. Avoid: Inconsistent button sizes. Severity: Medium. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Consistent button dimensions

name: html-tailwind: Touch targets
guidance: Minimum 44px touch target on mobile. Do: min-h-[44px] on mobile. Avoid: Small buttons on mobile. Severity: High. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Minimum 44px touch target on mobile

name: html-tailwind: Loading states
guidance: Show loading feedback. Do: disabled + spinner icon. Avoid: Clickable during loading. Severity: High. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Show loading feedback

name: html-tailwind: Icon buttons
guidance: Accessible icon-only buttons. Do: aria-label on icon buttons. Avoid: Icon button without label. Severity: High. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Accessible icon-only buttons

name: html-tailwind: Card structure
guidance: Consistent card styling. Do: rounded-lg shadow-md p-6. Avoid: Inconsistent card styles. Severity: Low. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Consistent card styling

name: html-tailwind: Card hover states
guidance: Interactive cards should have hover feedback. Do: hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow. Avoid: No hover on clickable cards. Severity: Medium. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Interactive cards should have hover feedback

name: html-tailwind: Card spacing
guidance: Consistent internal card spacing. Do: space-y-4 for card content. Avoid: Inconsistent internal spacing. Severity: Low. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Consistent internal card spacing

name: html-tailwind: Screen reader text
guidance: Provide context for screen readers. Do: sr-only for hidden labels. Avoid: Missing context for icons. Severity: High. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/screen-readers

details.Description: Provide context for screen readers

name: html-tailwind: Focus visible
guidance: Show focus only for keyboard users. Do: focus-visible:ring-2. Avoid: Focus on all interactions. Severity: Medium. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Show focus only for keyboard users

name: html-tailwind: Reduced motion
guidance: Respect user motion preferences. Do: motion-reduce:animate-none. Avoid: Ignore motion preferences. Severity: High. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/hover-focus-and-other-states#prefers-reduced-motion

details.Description: Respect user motion preferences

name: html-tailwind: Configure content paths
guidance: Tailwind needs to know where classes are used. Do: Use 'content' array in config. Avoid: Use deprecated 'purge' option (v2). Severity: High. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/content-configuration

details.Description: Tailwind needs to know where classes are used

name: html-tailwind: JIT mode
guidance: Use JIT for faster builds and smaller bundles. Do: JIT enabled (default in v3). Avoid: Full CSS in development. Severity: Medium. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Use JIT for faster builds and smaller bundles

name: html-tailwind: Avoid @apply bloat
guidance: Use @apply sparingly. Do: Direct utilities in HTML. Avoid: Heavy @apply usage. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/reusing-styles

details.Description: Use @apply sparingly

name: html-tailwind: Official plugins
guidance: Use official Tailwind plugins. Do: @tailwindcss/forms typography aspect-ratio. Avoid: Custom implementations. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/plugins

details.Description: Use official Tailwind plugins

name: html-tailwind: Custom utilities
guidance: Create utilities for repeated patterns. Do: Custom utility in config. Avoid: Repeated arbitrary values. Severity: Medium. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Create utilities for repeated patterns

name: html-tailwind: Container Queries
guidance: Use @container for component-based responsiveness. Do: Use @container and @lg: etc.. Avoid: Media queries for component internals. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss-container-queries

details.Description: Use @container for component-based responsiveness

name: html-tailwind: Group and Peer
guidance: Style based on parent/sibling state. Do: group-hover peer-checked. Avoid: JS for simple state interactions. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/hover-focus-and-other-states#styling-based-on-parent-state

details.Description: Style based on parent/sibling state

name: html-tailwind: Arbitrary Values
guidance: Use [] for one-off values. Do: w-[350px] for specific needs. Avoid: Creating config for single use. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/adding-custom-styles#using-arbitrary-values

details.Description: Use [] for one-off values

name: html-tailwind: Theme color variables
guidance: Define colors in Tailwind theme and use directly. Do: bg-primary text-success border-cta. Avoid: bg-[var(--color-primary)] text-[var(--color-success)]. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/customizing-colors

details.Description: Define colors in Tailwind theme and use directly

name: html-tailwind: Use bg-linear-to-* for gradients
guidance: Tailwind v4 uses bg-linear-to-* syntax for gradients. Do: bg-linear-to-r bg-linear-to-b. Avoid: bg-gradient-to-* (deprecated in v4). Severity: Medium. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/background-image

details.Description: Tailwind v4 uses bg-linear-to-* syntax for gradients

name: html-tailwind: Use shrink-0 shorthand
guidance: Shorter class name for flex-shrink-0. Do: shrink-0 shrink. Avoid: flex-shrink-0 flex-shrink. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/flex-shrink

details.Description: Shorter class name for flex-shrink-0

name: html-tailwind: Use size-* for square dimensions
guidance: Single utility for equal width and height. Do: size-4 size-8 size-12. Avoid: Separate h-* w-* for squares. Severity: Low. Docs: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/size

details.Description: Single utility for equal width and height

name: html-tailwind: SVG explicit dimensions
guidance: Add width/height attributes to SVGs to prevent layout shift before CSS loads. Do: <svg class='size-6' width='24' height='24'>. Avoid: SVG without explicit dimensions. Severity: High. Docs: n/a

details.Description: Add width/height attributes to SVGs to prevent layout shift before CSS loads

name: javafx: Start UI from Application subclass
guidance: JavaFX apps should bootstrap the primary Stage through Application.start(). Do: Extend Application and configure Scene in start(). Avoid: Create UI from a random main method without launching JavaFX. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/application/Application.html

details.Description: JavaFX apps should bootstrap the primary Stage through Application.start()
details.Do: Extend Application and configure Scene in start()
details.Don't: Create UI from a random main method without launching JavaFX
details.Code Good: public class App extends Application { public void start(Stage stage) { stage.setScene(new Scene(root)); stage.show(); } }

name: javafx: Keep work off the FX Application Thread
guidance: Long-running work blocks rendering and input when executed on the UI thread. Do: Use Task or Service for background work. Avoid: Run network database or file work in button handlers. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/concurrent/Task.html

details.Guideline: Keep work off the FX Application Thread
details.Description: Long-running work blocks rendering and input when executed on the UI thread
details.Do: Use Task or Service for background work
details.Don't: Run network database or file work in button handlers
details.Code Good: Task<List<Item>> task = new Task<>() { protected List<Item> call() { return repo.load(); } }; new Thread(task).start();

name: javafx: Update UI only on FX thread
guidance: Scene graph changes must happen on the JavaFX Application Thread. Do: Use bindings task handlers or Platform.runLater for UI changes. Avoid: Mutate controls directly from background threads. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/application/Platform.html

details.Guideline: Update UI only on FX thread
details.Description: Scene graph changes must happen on the JavaFX Application Thread
details.Do: Use bindings task handlers or Platform.runLater for UI changes
details.Don't: Mutate controls directly from background threads

name: javafx: Bind progress to background tasks
guidance: Task exposes progress and message properties for responsive feedback. Do: Bind ProgressBar and Label to task properties. Avoid: Poll progress manually or leave users without feedback. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/concurrent/Task.html

details.Description: Task exposes progress and message properties for responsive feedback
details.Do: Bind ProgressBar and Label to task properties
details.Don't: Poll progress manually or leave users without feedback

name: javafx: Use FXML for stable declarative layouts
guidance: FXML keeps view structure readable for screens with many controls. Do: Place layout in FXML and behavior in controller. Avoid: Build large screens entirely in one Java method. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.fxml/javafx/fxml/FXMLLoader.html

details.Guideline: Use FXML for stable declarative layouts
details.Description: FXML keeps view structure readable for screens with many controls
details.Do: Place layout in FXML and behavior in controller
details.Don't: Build large screens entirely in one Java method
details.Code Bad: VBox root = new VBox(); root.getChildren().add(... 200 lines ...);

name: javafx: Keep controllers focused on view behavior
guidance: Controllers should coordinate controls and delegate business logic to services. Do: Inject services or call application services from controller. Avoid: Put database queries and domain rules directly in controller. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.fxml/javafx/fxml/FXML.html

details.Guideline: Keep controllers focused on view behavior
details.Description: Controllers should coordinate controls and delegate business logic to services
details.Do: Inject services or call application services from controller
details.Don't: Put database queries and domain rules directly in controller

name: javafx: Use fx:id for injected controls
guidance: FXML controls need stable fx:id values that match controller fields. Do: Annotate fields with @FXML and keep ids descriptive. Avoid: Look up controls by CSS selector for normal wiring. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.fxml/javafx/fxml/FXML.html

details.Description: FXML controls need stable fx:id values that match controller fields
details.Do: Annotate fields with @FXML and keep ids descriptive
details.Don't: Look up controls by CSS selector for normal wiring

name: javafx: Fail fast when loading FXML
guidance: FXML load errors should surface during screen creation with clear context. Do: Load resources with getResource and handle IOException explicitly. Avoid: Swallow loader errors and show a blank scene. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.fxml/javafx/fxml/FXMLLoader.html

details.Description: FXML load errors should surface during screen creation with clear context
details.Do: Load resources with getResource and handle IOException explicitly
details.Don't: Swallow loader errors and show a blank scene

name: javafx: Style with style classes
guidance: JavaFX CSS works best through reusable styleClass names. Do: Add semantic style classes and define them in CSS. Avoid: Set long inline style strings throughout code. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/doc-files/cssref.html

details.Description: JavaFX CSS works best through reusable styleClass names
details.Do: Add semantic style classes and define them in CSS
details.Don't: Set long inline style strings throughout code

name: javafx: Use design tokens through looked-up colors
guidance: Looked-up colors keep palettes consistent across controls. Do: Define named colors on root and reuse them in CSS. Avoid: Repeat hex values in every selector. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/doc-files/cssref.html

details.Guideline: Use design tokens through looked-up colors
details.Description: Looked-up colors keep palettes consistent across controls
details.Do: Define named colors on root and reuse them in CSS
details.Don't: Repeat hex values in every selector

name: javafx: Avoid overusing inline effects
guidance: Expensive CSS effects and shadows can hurt desktop UI responsiveness. Do: Use subtle shadows only on important elevated surfaces. Avoid: Apply blur drop shadow and glow to every node. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/effect/package-summary.html

details.Description: Expensive CSS effects and shadows can hurt desktop UI responsiveness
details.Do: Use subtle shadows only on important elevated surfaces
details.Don't: Apply blur drop shadow and glow to every node

name: javafx: Choose layout panes by responsibility
guidance: Each pane solves a different layout problem and should be selected intentionally. Do: Use BorderPane for app shell GridPane for forms VBox/HBox for simple stacks. Avoid: Use absolute positioning for resizable app screens. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/layout/package-summary.html

details.Description: Each pane solves a different layout problem and should be selected intentionally
details.Do: Use BorderPane for app shell GridPane for forms VBox/HBox for simple stacks
details.Don't: Use absolute positioning for resizable app screens
details.Code Good: BorderPane shell = new BorderPane(); shell.setTop(toolbar); shell.setCenter(content);

name: javafx: Prefer constraints over fixed coordinates
guidance: Responsive JavaFX layouts depend on constraints and grow priorities. Do: Use hgrow vgrow column constraints and alignment. Avoid: Hard-code pixel positions and sizes. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/layout/GridPane.html

details.Description: Responsive JavaFX layouts depend on constraints and grow priorities
details.Do: Use hgrow vgrow column constraints and alignment

name: javafx: Set sensible min pref and max sizes
guidance: Controls should resize predictably across windows and DPI settings. Do: Use Region.USE_COMPUTED_SIZE and max widths intentionally. Avoid: Lock every control to fixed width and height. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/layout/Region.html

details.Guideline: Set sensible min pref and max sizes
details.Description: Controls should resize predictably across windows and DPI settings
details.Do: Use Region.USE_COMPUTED_SIZE and max widths intentionally
details.Don't: Lock every control to fixed width and height

name: javafx: Use spacing and padding consistently
guidance: Desktop UI needs scan-friendly rhythm and clear grouping. Do: Set spacing padding and Insets through shared constants or CSS. Avoid: Use inconsistent ad hoc gaps between controls. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/geometry/Insets.html

details.Description: Desktop UI needs scan-friendly rhythm and clear grouping
details.Do: Set spacing padding and Insets through shared constants or CSS
details.Don't: Use inconsistent ad hoc gaps between controls

name: javafx: Use ObservableList for list controls
guidance: TableView ListView and ComboBox update automatically from observable collections. Do: Back controls with FXCollections.observableArrayList(). Avoid: Mutate plain lists and manually refresh controls. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/collections/ObservableList.html

details.Description: TableView ListView and ComboBox update automatically from observable collections
details.Don't: Mutate plain lists and manually refresh controls
details.Code Bad: List<Customer> rows = new ArrayList<>(); table.setItems((ObservableList) rows);

name: javafx: Configure TableView cell value factories with properties
guidance: Table columns should observe stable JavaFX properties for updates. Do: Expose StringProperty ObjectProperty or use ReadOnlyObjectWrapper. Avoid: Return transient strings without observable support. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/TableColumn.html

details.Guideline: Configure TableView cell value factories with properties
details.Description: Table columns should observe stable JavaFX properties for updates
details.Do: Expose StringProperty ObjectProperty or use ReadOnlyObjectWrapper
details.Don't: Return transient strings without observable support

name: javafx: Use cell factories for custom rendering
guidance: Custom table or list visuals belong in reusable cell factories. Do: Override updateItem and handle empty state. Avoid: Place complex Nodes directly in model objects. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/Cell.html

details.Guideline: Use cell factories for custom rendering
details.Description: Custom table or list visuals belong in reusable cell factories
details.Do: Override updateItem and handle empty state
details.Don't: Place complex Nodes directly in model objects
details.Code Good: col.setCellFactory(c -> new TableCell<>() { protected void updateItem(Status s, boolean empty) { super.updateItem(s, empty); setText(empty ? null : s.label()); } });

name: javafx: Virtualized controls are for large data
guidance: TableView ListView TreeView virtualize cells and outperform manual node lists. Do: Use TableView or ListView for hundreds of rows. Avoid: Create hundreds of HBoxes inside a VBox. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/ListView.html

details.Guideline: Virtualized controls are for large data
details.Description: TableView ListView TreeView virtualize cells and outperform manual node lists
details.Do: Use TableView or ListView for hundreds of rows
details.Don't: Create hundreds of HBoxes inside a VBox

name: javafx: Handle empty states explicitly
guidance: Empty tables and lists need visible guidance or next actions. Do: Set placeholder nodes for empty data views. Avoid: Leave blank white areas that look broken. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/TableView.html

details.Description: Empty tables and lists need visible guidance or next actions
details.Do: Set placeholder nodes for empty data views
details.Don't: Leave blank white areas that look broken
details.Code Good: table.setPlaceholder(new Label(\No customers match this filter\));

name: javafx: Use property binding for derived UI state
guidance: JavaFX binding reduces imperative synchronization bugs. Do: Bind disabled visible text and progress properties to source state. Avoid: Manually update every dependent control in each event handler. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/beans/binding/Bindings.html

details.Guideline: Use property binding for derived UI state
details.Description: JavaFX binding reduces imperative synchronization bugs
details.Do: Bind disabled visible text and progress properties to source state
details.Don't: Manually update every dependent control in each event handler

name: javafx: Unbind before manual updates
guidance: Bound properties cannot be set directly without errors. Do: Call unbind when switching from bound to manual state. Avoid: Set a bound property directly. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/beans/property/Property.html

details.Description: Bound properties cannot be set directly without errors
details.Do: Call unbind when switching from bound to manual state

name: javafx: Use listeners sparingly
guidance: Bindings express simple relationships more clearly than listeners. Do: Use listeners for side effects and bindings for values. Avoid: Create listener chains for simple computed text. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/beans/value/ObservableValue.html

details.Description: Bindings express simple relationships more clearly than listeners
details.Do: Use listeners for side effects and bindings for values
details.Don't: Create listener chains for simple computed text

name: javafx: Use action handlers for commands
guidance: Buttons and menu items should route to named command methods. Do: Use setOnAction or @FXML handler methods with clear names. Avoid: Put large lambdas inline for complex operations. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/event/ActionEvent.html

details.Description: Buttons and menu items should route to named command methods
details.Do: Use setOnAction or @FXML handler methods with clear names
details.Don't: Put large lambdas inline for complex operations
details.Code Bad: saveButton.setOnAction(e -> { validate(); transform(); query(); save(); refresh(); });

name: javafx: Use event filters for global shortcuts
guidance: Filters can intercept keyboard events before child controls consume them. Do: Register accelerators or filters at Scene level. Avoid: Add duplicate key handlers to every control. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/Scene.html

details.Guideline: Use event filters for global shortcuts
details.Description: Filters can intercept keyboard events before child controls consume them
details.Do: Register accelerators or filters at Scene level
details.Don't: Add duplicate key handlers to every control

name: javafx: Connect labels to inputs
guidance: Accessible desktop forms need labels associated with controls. Do: Use Label.setLabelFor and clear prompt text. Avoid: Use placeholder-only labels. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/Label.html

details.Description: Accessible desktop forms need labels associated with controls
details.Do: Use Label.setLabelFor and clear prompt text

name: javafx: Expose accessible text for icon buttons
guidance: Icon-only controls need names for screen readers and tooltips. Do: Set accessibleText and Tooltip on icon buttons. Avoid: Use unlabeled graphic-only buttons. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/AccessibleRole.html

details.Guideline: Expose accessible text for icon buttons
details.Description: Icon-only controls need names for screen readers and tooltips
details.Do: Set accessibleText and Tooltip on icon buttons

name: javafx: Keep keyboard focus visible
guidance: Desktop users rely on focus traversal and visible focus indicators. Do: Preserve focus rings and tab order. Avoid: Remove outlines without alternative focus state. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/Node.html

details.Description: Desktop users rely on focus traversal and visible focus indicators
details.Do: Preserve focus rings and tab order
details.Don't: Remove outlines without alternative focus state

name: javafx: Use mnemonics for menu and form workflows
guidance: Mnemonics make desktop workflows faster and more accessible. Do: Enable mnemonicParsing and choose unique mnemonic letters. Avoid: Ignore keyboard alternatives for frequent actions. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/Labeled.html

details.Guideline: Use mnemonics for menu and form workflows
details.Description: Mnemonics make desktop workflows faster and more accessible
details.Do: Enable mnemonicParsing and choose unique mnemonic letters
details.Don't: Ignore keyboard alternatives for frequent actions

name: javafx: Show validation near the field
guidance: Users should not hunt for form errors in desktop dialogs. Do: Bind error labels or pseudo classes next to invalid controls. Avoid: Show only a generic alert after submit. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/css/PseudoClass.html

details.Description: Users should not hunt for form errors in desktop dialogs
details.Do: Bind error labels or pseudo classes next to invalid controls
details.Don't: Show only a generic alert after submit

name: javafx: Use TextFormatter for constrained input
guidance: TextFormatter prevents invalid edits before they enter the model. Do: Attach TextFormatter for numeric dates and masks. Avoid: Parse and reject invalid text only after submit. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/TextFormatter.html

details.Description: TextFormatter prevents invalid edits before they enter the model
details.Do: Attach TextFormatter for numeric dates and masks
details.Don't: Parse and reject invalid text only after submit
details.Code Good: amountField.setTextFormatter(new TextFormatter<>(new IntegerStringConverter(), 0, c -> c.getControlNewText().matches("\\d*") ? c : null));

name: javafx: Use modal ownership for dialogs
guidance: Dialogs should block only the relevant window and return structured results. Do: Set owner modality and use showAndWait. Avoid: Open unmanaged windows for confirmations. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/stage/Modality.html

details.Description: Dialogs should block only the relevant window and return structured results
details.Do: Set owner modality and use showAndWait

name: javafx: Prefer custom DialogPane over ad hoc stages
guidance: Dialog gives consistent buttons focus and result handling. Do: Use Dialog<T> for forms confirmations and wizards. Avoid: Build every modal as a new Stage manually. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.controls/javafx/scene/control/Dialog.html

details.Guideline: Prefer custom DialogPane over ad hoc stages
details.Description: Dialog gives consistent buttons focus and result handling
details.Do: Use Dialog<T> for forms confirmations and wizards
details.Don't: Build every modal as a new Stage manually
details.Code Bad: Stage modal = new Stage(); modal.setScene(new Scene(new VBox()));

name: javafx: Load images as resources
guidance: Packaged apps need resources resolved from the classpath or module path. Do: Use getResourceAsStream for bundled assets. Avoid: Use absolute local file paths in production UI. Severity: High. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/image/Image.html

details.Description: Packaged apps need resources resolved from the classpath or module path
details.Don't: Use absolute local file paths in production UI

name: javafx: Use background loading for large images
guidance: Large image decoding can pause UI startup. Do: Use Image(url true) or a background Task for heavy assets. Avoid: Load many full-size images synchronously during startup. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/image/Image.html

details.Guideline: Use background loading for large images
details.Description: Large image decoding can pause UI startup
details.Do: Use Image(url true) or a background Task for heavy assets
details.Don't: Load many full-size images synchronously during startup

name: javafx: Keep animations purposeful and short
guidance: Desktop UI animations should clarify state changes without delaying work. Do: Use 150-250ms transitions for reveal hover and selection. Avoid: Animate every layout change with long timelines. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/animation/package-summary.html

details.Description: Desktop UI animations should clarify state changes without delaying work
details.Do: Use 150-250ms transitions for reveal hover and selection
details.Don't: Animate every layout change with long timelines
details.Code Good: FadeTransition ft = new FadeTransition(Duration.millis(180), pane); ft.setToValue(1);

name: javafx: Respect reduced-motion contexts where possible
guidance: Some users experience motion sensitivity in desktop apps. Do: Provide a setting to disable decorative animations. Avoid: Make animation required for comprehension. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/animation/Animation.html

details.Description: Some users experience motion sensitivity in desktop apps
details.Do: Provide a setting to disable decorative animations

name: javafx: Avoid recreating scenes for small state changes
guidance: Replacing whole scenes loses state and can flicker. Do: Swap center content or update view models. Avoid: Rebuild the entire Stage for every navigation click. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.graphics/javafx/scene/Scene.html

details.Guideline: Avoid recreating scenes for small state changes
details.Description: Replacing whole scenes loses state and can flicker
details.Do: Swap center content or update view models
details.Don't: Rebuild the entire Stage for every navigation click

name: javafx: Reuse loaded views when appropriate
guidance: FXML loading and CSS application are not free. Do: Cache stable views or controllers for frequent navigation. Avoid: Reload heavyweight screens repeatedly without need. Severity: Low. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.fxml/javafx/fxml/FXMLLoader.html

details.Description: FXML loading and CSS application are not free
details.Do: Cache stable views or controllers for frequent navigation
details.Don't: Reload heavyweight screens repeatedly without need

name: javafx: Batch observable list changes
guidance: Many single-item updates can cause repeated layout and sort work. Do: Use setAll or addAll for bulk replacement. Avoid: Loop add items one by one to visible lists. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/collections/ObservableList.html

details.Description: Many single-item updates can cause repeated layout and sort work
details.Do: Use setAll or addAll for bulk replacement
details.Don't: Loop add items one by one to visible lists

name: javafx: Use view models for complex screens
guidance: View models keep controller state testable and separate from controls. Do: Expose JavaFX properties from a screen model. Avoid: Store all state only inside controls. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://openjfx.io/javadoc/21/javafx.base/javafx/beans/property/package-summary.html

details.Guideline: Use view models for complex screens
details.Description: View models keep controller state testable and separate from controls
details.Do: Expose JavaFX properties from a screen model
details.Don't: Store all state only inside controls

name: javafx: Separate navigation from feature controllers
