name: threejs: CDN Version Lock
guidance: Always use Three.js r128 from cdnjs. It is the stable CDN baseline. Never use a floating 'latest' URL — it silently breaks when the CDN updates without warning.. Do: Pin to the exact r128 cdnjs URL in every HTML file. Avoid: Use unpkg@latest or any unversioned CDN URL that can silently update. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/three.min.js

details.Description: Always use Three.js r128 from cdnjs. It is the stable CDN baseline. Never use a floating 'latest' URL — it silently breaks when the CDN updates without warning.
details.Do: Pin to the exact r128 cdnjs URL in every HTML file
details.Don't: Use unpkg@latest or any unversioned CDN URL that can silently update

name: threejs: CapsuleGeometry Does Not Exist in r128
guidance: THREE.CapsuleGeometry was introduced in r142. Using it on the r128 CDN throws 'CapsuleGeometry is not a constructor' and crashes the entire scene. Build a capsule from primitives instead.. Do: Build a capsule from CylinderGeometry plus two SphereGeometry end caps. Avoid: Call THREE.CapsuleGeometry on r128 — it is undefined and throws immediately. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/geometries/CapsuleGeometry

details.Guideline: CapsuleGeometry Does Not Exist in r128
details.Description: THREE.CapsuleGeometry was introduced in r142. Using it on the r128 CDN throws 'CapsuleGeometry is not a constructor' and crashes the entire scene. Build a capsule from primitives instead.
details.Do: Build a capsule from CylinderGeometry plus two SphereGeometry end caps
details.Don't: Call THREE.CapsuleGeometry on r128 — it is undefined and throws immediately
details.Code Good: const body = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.5, 0.5, 1, 16), mat); const topCap = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.5, 16, 8), mat); const botCap = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.5, 16, 8), mat); topCap.position.y = 0.5; botCap.position.y = -0.5; const group = new THREE.Group(); group.add(body, topCap, botCap);
details.Code Bad: const cap = new THREE.CapsuleGeometry(0.5, 1, 4, 8); // TypeError: CapsuleGeometry is not a constructor on r128

name: threejs: OrbitControls Must Be Loaded Separately
guidance: OrbitControls is NOT bundled in the core Three.js r128 CDN file. It lives in examples/js and must be loaded from a separate cdnjs script tag. THREE.OrbitControls is undefined without it.. Do: Load the OrbitControls script from cdnjs examples path before your scene script. Avoid: Expect THREE.OrbitControls to exist after loading only the core Three.js CDN script. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://cdnjs.com/libraries/three.js/r128

details.Description: OrbitControls is NOT bundled in the core Three.js r128 CDN file. It lives in examples/js and must be loaded from a separate cdnjs script tag. THREE.OrbitControls is undefined without it.
details.Do: Load the OrbitControls script from cdnjs examples path before your scene script
details.Don't: Expect THREE.OrbitControls to exist after loading only the core Three.js CDN script

name: threejs: Custom Drag Orbit Fallback
guidance: When OrbitControls cannot be loaded implement spherical orbit using mousedown/mousemove/mouseup. The key is rotating in spherical coordinates so both horizontal AND vertical drag work correctly.. Do: Rotate camera in spherical coordinates so both axes respond correctly to drag. Avoid: Move camera.position.x directly — vertical drag is silently ignored and the orbit is incorrect. Severity: High. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent

details.Description: When OrbitControls cannot be loaded implement spherical orbit using mousedown/mousemove/mouseup. The key is rotating in spherical coordinates so both horizontal AND vertical drag work correctly.
details.Do: Rotate camera in spherical coordinates so both axes respond correctly to drag
details.Don't: Move camera.position.x directly — vertical drag is silently ignored and the orbit is incorrect
details.Code Good: let dragging = false; let prev = { x: 0, y: 0 }; const radius = camera.position.length(); let theta = 0; let phi = Math.PI / 2; canvas.addEventListener('mousedown', () => dragging = true); canvas.addEventListener('mouseup', () => dragging = false); canvas.addEventListener('mousemove', e => { if (!dragging) return; theta -= (e.clientX - prev.x) * 0.005; phi = Math.max(0.1, Math.min(Math.PI - 0.1, phi - (e.clientY - prev.y) * 0.005)); camera.position.set(radius * Math.sin(phi) * Math.sin(theta), radius * Math.cos(phi), radius * Math.sin(phi) * Math.cos(theta)); camera.lookAt(scene.position); prev = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; });
details.Code Bad: let dragging = false; let prev = { x: 0, y: 0 }; canvas.addEventListener('mousemove', e => { if (!dragging) return; camera.position.x += (e.clientX - prev.x) * 0.005; camera.lookAt(scene.position); prev = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; }); // BUG: Y-drag ignored; orbit is a horizontal slide not a sphere

name: threejs: ESM vs CDN Import
guidance: When using a bundler import Three.js as an ES module. When using CDN the THREE global is already available — do not import it again. Mixing both loads Three.js twice and causes subtle runtime errors.. Do: Match import style to build environment: ESM import for bundlers; rely on the window.THREE global for CDN pages. Avoid: Mix a CDN script tag with an ES module import in the same file. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#manual/en/introduction/Installation

details.Description: When using a bundler import Three.js as an ES module. When using CDN the THREE global is already available — do not import it again. Mixing both loads Three.js twice and causes subtle runtime errors.
details.Do: Match import style to build environment: ESM import for bundlers; rely on the window.THREE global for CDN pages
details.Don't: Mix a CDN script tag with an ES module import in the same file
details.Code Good: // Bundler project (Vite / webpack): import * as THREE from 'three'; // CDN project — no import needed; THREE is already global after the script tag
details.Code Bad: <!-- CDN script --> <script src="r128.cdn"></script> <script type="module"> import * as THREE from 'three'; // loads Three.js twice — version mismatch risk </script>

name: threejs: Single Renderer Per Page
guidance: Create one WebGLRenderer instance for the lifetime of the page. Multiple renderers compete for the browser GPU context limit (8–16 contexts) and cause context-lost errors especially on mobile.. Do: Reuse a single renderer and swap scene content instead of recreating the renderer. Avoid: Create a new renderer on each component mount or scene transition. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer

details.Description: Create one WebGLRenderer instance for the lifetime of the page. Multiple renderers compete for the browser GPU context limit (8–16 contexts) and cause context-lost errors especially on mobile.
details.Do: Reuse a single renderer and swap scene content instead of recreating the renderer
details.Don't: Create a new renderer on each component mount or scene transition
details.Code Good: const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ antialias: true, alpha: true }); renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2)); renderer.setSize(canvas.clientWidth, canvas.clientHeight); // renderer lives for the page lifetime
details.Code Bad: function showScene() { const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer(); document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement); } showScene(); showScene(); // two GPU contexts — crashes on mobile

name: threejs: Pixel Ratio Cap at 2
guidance: Cap devicePixelRatio at 2. Retina displays report 3x or higher. Going from 2x to 3x multiplies pixel count by 2.25x with no visible quality improvement at normal viewing distance.. Do: Apply Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) — cap is at 2 not at 3. Avoid: Pass window.devicePixelRatio directly without any cap. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.setPixelRatio

details.Description: Cap devicePixelRatio at 2. Retina displays report 3x or higher. Going from 2x to 3x multiplies pixel count by 2.25x with no visible quality improvement at normal viewing distance.
details.Do: Apply Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) — cap is at 2 not at 3
details.Don't: Pass window.devicePixelRatio directly without any cap
details.Code Bad: renderer.setPixelRatio(window.devicePixelRatio); // 3x display = 9 pixels per CSS pixel = 2.25x GPU cost for zero quality gain

name: threejs: Alpha Canvas Plus CSS Background
guidance: Set alpha:true on the renderer and control the background color through CSS rather than a renderer clear color. This composites the canvas correctly over any HTML content behind it.. Do: Set alpha:true on renderer and let body or a parent div provide the background color. Avoid: Set a solid renderer clear color when the canvas must composite over HTML behind it. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.setClearColor

details.Description: Set alpha:true on the renderer and control the background color through CSS rather than a renderer clear color. This composites the canvas correctly over any HTML content behind it.
details.Do: Set alpha:true on renderer and let body or a parent div provide the background color
details.Don't: Set a solid renderer clear color when the canvas must composite over HTML behind it
details.Code Good: const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ alpha: true }); renderer.setClearColor(0x000000, 0); // fully transparent canvas // body { background: #0d0d0d; } handles the visible color
details.Code Bad: renderer.setClearColor(0x111827); // fully opaque — HTML behind the canvas is blocked

name: threejs: Aspect Ratio on Resize
guidance: Always update camera.aspect and call camera.updateProjectionMatrix() inside every resize handler. A stale aspect ratio causes the entire scene to appear stretched or squashed horizontally.. Do: Update camera.aspect then call updateProjectionMatrix() on every resize. Avoid: Let aspect ratio become stale after the browser window changes size. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/cameras/PerspectiveCamera

details.Description: Always update camera.aspect and call camera.updateProjectionMatrix() inside every resize handler. A stale aspect ratio causes the entire scene to appear stretched or squashed horizontally.
details.Do: Update camera.aspect then call updateProjectionMatrix() on every resize
details.Don't: Let aspect ratio become stale after the browser window changes size
details.Code Good: window.addEventListener('resize', () => { camera.aspect = canvas.clientWidth / canvas.clientHeight; camera.updateProjectionMatrix(); renderer.setSize(canvas.clientWidth, canvas.clientHeight); });
details.Code Bad: // No resize handler — scene stretches to fill a wider window without correcting the projection

name: threejs: FOV Range 45 to 75
guidance: Use a field of view between 45 and 75 degrees. Below 45 creates compressed telephoto distortion. Above 90 creates visible fisheye distortion at frame edges.. Do: Start at 75 for general interactive scenes; use 45–55 for product close-ups. Avoid: Use FOV above 90 or below 30 without a deliberate artistic reason. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/cameras/PerspectiveCamera

details.Description: Use a field of view between 45 and 75 degrees. Below 45 creates compressed telephoto distortion. Above 90 creates visible fisheye distortion at frame edges.
details.Do: Start at 75 for general interactive scenes; use 45–55 for product close-ups
details.Don't: Use FOV above 90 or below 30 without a deliberate artistic reason
details.Code Good: const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(75, aspect, 0.1, 1000); // general const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(50, aspect, 0.1, 1000); // product shot
details.Code Bad: const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(120, aspect, 0.1, 1000); // fisheye distortion at all edges

name: threejs: Explicit Position and lookAt
guidance: Always set an explicit camera position and call camera.lookAt() before the first render. The default camera at the origin pointing down -Z makes subjects at arbitrary coordinates invisible or tiny.. Do: Set camera.position.set() and camera.lookAt() to frame the subject before the first render. Avoid: Leave the camera at default position (0 0 0) with no lookAt — subject may be behind the camera or microscopic. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/cameras/Camera.lookAt

details.Description: Always set an explicit camera position and call camera.lookAt() before the first render. The default camera at the origin pointing down -Z makes subjects at arbitrary coordinates invisible or tiny.
details.Do: Set camera.position.set() and camera.lookAt() to frame the subject before the first render
details.Don't: Leave the camera at default position (0 0 0) with no lookAt — subject may be behind the camera or microscopic
details.Code Bad: // No position or lookAt set — subject at y:2 is behind or above the default camera view

name: threejs: OrbitControls vs GSAP Camera Rig
guidance: Use OrbitControls for model viewers and exploratory scenes where the user needs free-look. Use a GSAP scroll-driven camera rig for product reveals or storytelling where the camera path must stay fixed.. Do: Match camera control approach to the UX intent of the scene. Avoid: Use OrbitControls for a scripted reveal — users can orbit away from the reveal before it completes. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#examples/en/controls/OrbitControls

details.Description: Use OrbitControls for model viewers and exploratory scenes where the user needs free-look. Use a GSAP scroll-driven camera rig for product reveals or storytelling where the camera path must stay fixed.
details.Do: Match camera control approach to the UX intent of the scene
details.Don't: Use OrbitControls for a scripted reveal — users can orbit away from the reveal before it completes
details.Code Good: // Scroll storytelling — GSAP controls the path: gsap.to(camera.position, { z: 2, scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.scene', scrub: 1 } }); // Free-look model viewer — OrbitControls: const controls = new THREE.OrbitControls(camera, renderer.domElement); controls.enableDamping = true; // call controls.update() in animate()
details.Code Bad: // Scripted product reveal: const controls = new THREE.OrbitControls(camera, renderer.domElement); // user can rotate away before the animation completes

name: threejs: Never Create Geometry Per Frame
guidance: Creating a new geometry inside animate() allocates a fresh GPU buffer every frame and exhausts VRAM within seconds. Create all geometry exactly once before the loop starts. Use attribute mutation if positions must change per frame.. Do: Create all geometry before the animation loop; mutate BufferAttribute arrays in-place if needed. Avoid: Call any new XxxGeometry() constructor inside the animation loop. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/BufferGeometry

details.Description: Creating a new geometry inside animate() allocates a fresh GPU buffer every frame and exhausts VRAM within seconds. Create all geometry exactly once before the loop starts. Use attribute mutation if positions must change per frame.
details.Do: Create all geometry before the animation loop; mutate BufferAttribute arrays in-place if needed
details.Don't: Call any new XxxGeometry() constructor inside the animation loop
details.Code Good: const geo = new THREE.SphereGeometry(1, 32, 32); // created once const mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(mesh); const clock = new THREE.Clock(); function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); mesh.rotation.y += clock.getDelta() * 0.8; // delta time renderer.render(scene, camera); }
details.Code Bad: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); const geo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(1, 1, 1); // NEW GPU buffer every frame — VRAM exhaustion }

name: threejs: Share Geometry Across Meshes
guidance: When multiple objects share the same shape create one geometry instance and pass it to every Mesh. Each Mesh gets its own transform and material while all share a single GPU buffer.. Do: Create one geometry and pass the same reference to every Mesh constructor. Avoid: Create a separate identical geometry inside a loop for each object. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/BufferGeometry

details.Description: When multiple objects share the same shape create one geometry instance and pass it to every Mesh. Each Mesh gets its own transform and material while all share a single GPU buffer.
details.Do: Create one geometry and pass the same reference to every Mesh constructor
details.Don't: Create a separate identical geometry inside a loop for each object
details.Code Good: const geo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(1, 1, 1); // one GPU buffer for (let i = 0; i < 200; i++) { const m = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); m.position.set(Math.random() * 10, 0, Math.random() * 10); scene.add(m); }
details.Code Bad: for (let i = 0; i < 200; i++) { const geo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(1, 1, 1); // 200 separate GPU buffers scene.add(new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat)); }

name: threejs: dispose on Scene Removal
guidance: Call geometry.dispose() and material.dispose() and texture.dispose() for every texture map when removing objects from the scene. Three.js never releases GPU resources automatically — they stay in VRAM until explicitly freed.. Do: Dispose of geometry + material + every texture map before calling scene.remove(). Avoid: Call scene.remove() alone without any dispose calls. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/BufferGeometry.dispose

details.Description: Call geometry.dispose() and material.dispose() and texture.dispose() for every texture map when removing objects from the scene. Three.js never releases GPU resources automatically — they stay in VRAM until explicitly freed.
details.Do: Dispose of geometry + material + every texture map before calling scene.remove()
details.Don't: Call scene.remove() alone without any dispose calls
details.Code Good: function removeMesh(mesh) { scene.remove(mesh); mesh.geometry.dispose(); if (mesh.material.map) mesh.material.map.dispose(); if (mesh.material.normalMap) mesh.material.normalMap.dispose(); mesh.material.dispose(); }
details.Code Bad: scene.remove(mesh); // geometry and all texture maps stay in GPU VRAM forever

name: threejs: Segment Count Budget
guidance: Use the minimum segment count that achieves the desired silhouette quality. Hero objects: 32–64 segments. Background objects: 8–16. Particle stand-ins: 6–8. High counts on background geometry waste GPU draw calls with zero visible benefit.. Do: Apply a tiered segment budget based on the visual priority of each object in the scene. Avoid: Default every sphere and cylinder to 64+ segments regardless of its role. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/geometries/SphereGeometry

details.Description: Use the minimum segment count that achieves the desired silhouette quality. Hero objects: 32–64 segments. Background objects: 8–16. Particle stand-ins: 6–8. High counts on background geometry waste GPU draw calls with zero visible benefit.
details.Do: Apply a tiered segment budget based on the visual priority of each object in the scene
details.Don't: Default every sphere and cylinder to 64+ segments regardless of its role
details.Code Good: const bgSphere = new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.5, 8, 8); // background const heroSphere = new THREE.SphereGeometry(1, 64, 64); // foreground product
details.Code Bad: const particleSphere = new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.1, 64, 64); // 64 segments × 1000 particles = massive overdraw

name: threejs: BufferGeometry for Custom Vertex Data
guidance: For any custom shape use BufferGeometry with setAttribute('position' ...) and a Float32Array. The legacy THREE.Geometry class was removed in r125 and throws ReferenceError in r128.. Do: Use THREE.BufferGeometry with a Float32Array position attribute for custom vertex data. Avoid: Reference or instantiate the removed THREE.Geometry class. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/BufferGeometry

details.Description: For any custom shape use BufferGeometry with setAttribute('position' ...) and a Float32Array. The legacy THREE.Geometry class was removed in r125 and throws ReferenceError in r128.
details.Do: Use THREE.BufferGeometry with a Float32Array position attribute for custom vertex data
details.Don't: Reference or instantiate the removed THREE.Geometry class
details.Code Good: const geo = new THREE.BufferGeometry(); geo.setAttribute('position', new THREE.BufferAttribute(new Float32Array(vertices), 3)); geo.setAttribute('normal', new THREE.BufferAttribute(new Float32Array(normals), 3));
details.Code Bad: const geo = new THREE.Geometry(); geo.vertices.push(new THREE.Vector3(0, 0, 0)); // ReferenceError: Geometry is not defined in r128

name: threejs: MeshBasicMaterial vs MeshStandardMaterial
guidance: MeshBasicMaterial ignores all lights and is significantly cheaper — use it for UI overlays HUDs and flat-colored decorative elements. MeshStandardMaterial is PBR-accurate and requires lights. Never use StandardMaterial where BasicMaterial suffices.. Do: Use MeshBasicMaterial for any object that does not need lighting; use MeshStandardMaterial for physical objects. Avoid: Apply MeshStandardMaterial to flat UI elements that never receive light — lights still run for them. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/materials/MeshStandardMaterial

details.Description: MeshBasicMaterial ignores all lights and is significantly cheaper — use it for UI overlays HUDs and flat-colored decorative elements. MeshStandardMaterial is PBR-accurate and requires lights. Never use StandardMaterial where BasicMaterial suffices.
details.Do: Use MeshBasicMaterial for any object that does not need lighting; use MeshStandardMaterial for physical objects
details.Don't: Apply MeshStandardMaterial to flat UI elements that never receive light — lights still run for them
details.Code Good: const uiMat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0xffffff }); // no lighting cost const physMat = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x4f46e5, roughness: 0.4, metalness: 0.6 });
details.Code Bad: const mat = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0xffffff }); // on a 2D HUD card — lighting calculation runs with no visual benefit

name: threejs: Share Material Instances
guidance: Share one material instance across all meshes that have identical properties. Call mat.clone() only when individual meshes genuinely need different property values. Duplicate materials waste GPU VRAM.. Do: Assign the same material reference to all meshes with identical visual properties. Avoid: Create a new material inside a loop for objects that look identical. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/materials/Material

details.Description: Share one material instance across all meshes that have identical properties. Call mat.clone() only when individual meshes genuinely need different property values. Duplicate materials waste GPU VRAM.
details.Do: Assign the same material reference to all meshes with identical visual properties
details.Don't: Create a new material inside a loop for objects that look identical
details.Code Good: const mat = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x4f46e5, roughness: 0.5 }); meshA.material = mat; meshB.material = mat; meshC.material = mat; // one GPU material
details.Code Bad: for (const m of meshes) { m.material = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x4f46e5 }); } // N redundant GPU materials

name: threejs: Dispose Textures Explicitly
guidance: Textures are the single largest consumer of GPU VRAM in most Three.js scenes. Call texture.dispose() when switching scenes or removing objects — Three.js does not garbage-collect GPU resources automatically.. Do: Track all loaded textures and call dispose() on each one during scene teardown or on object removal. Avoid: Load textures without any cleanup path — they persist in VRAM for the entire page lifetime. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/textures/Texture.dispose

details.Description: Textures are the single largest consumer of GPU VRAM in most Three.js scenes. Call texture.dispose() when switching scenes or removing objects — Three.js does not garbage-collect GPU resources automatically.
details.Do: Track all loaded textures and call dispose() on each one during scene teardown or on object removal
details.Don't: Load textures without any cleanup path — they persist in VRAM for the entire page lifetime
details.Code Good: const tex = new THREE.TextureLoader().load('img.jpg'); mesh.material.map = tex; // on teardown: tex.dispose(); mesh.material.dispose();
details.Code Bad: const tex = new THREE.TextureLoader().load('img.jpg'); scene.remove(mesh); // tex occupies GPU VRAM until page reload

name: threejs: Ambient Plus Directional Minimum
guidance: Any scene using MeshStandardMaterial or MeshPhongMaterial requires at minimum one AmbientLight (fill) and one DirectionalLight (shading direction). Without both the objects render as solid black — the material is there but no light reaches it.. Do: Add AmbientLight for fill and DirectionalLight for shading whenever PBR or Phong materials are used. Avoid: Use MeshStandardMaterial without adding any lights to the scene. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/lights/DirectionalLight

details.Description: Any scene using MeshStandardMaterial or MeshPhongMaterial requires at minimum one AmbientLight (fill) and one DirectionalLight (shading direction). Without both the objects render as solid black — the material is there but no light reaches it.
details.Do: Add AmbientLight for fill and DirectionalLight for shading whenever PBR or Phong materials are used
details.Don't: Use MeshStandardMaterial without adding any lights to the scene
details.Code Good: scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.4)); const dirLight = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 1.0); dirLight.position.set(5, 10, 7.5); scene.add(dirLight);
details.Code Bad: const mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geo, new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x4f46e5 })); scene.add(mesh); // renders completely black — no lights in scene

name: threejs: Enable shadowMap Before castShadow
guidance: renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true must be set before any castShadow or receiveShadow flags. Without it the shadow map is never allocated and all shadow flags are silently ignored.. Do: Set renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true first then set castShadow and receiveShadow on lights and meshes. Avoid: Set castShadow on a light or mesh without enabling renderer.shadowMap.enabled — shadows never render. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.shadowMap

details.Description: renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true must be set before any castShadow or receiveShadow flags. Without it the shadow map is never allocated and all shadow flags are silently ignored.
details.Do: Set renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true first then set castShadow and receiveShadow on lights and meshes
details.Don't: Set castShadow on a light or mesh without enabling renderer.shadowMap.enabled — shadows never render
details.Code Good: renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true; renderer.shadowMap.type = THREE.PCFSoftShadowMap; dirLight.castShadow = true; dirLight.shadow.mapSize.width = 2048; dirLight.shadow.mapSize.height = 2048; heroMesh.castShadow = true; ground.receiveShadow = true;
details.Code Bad: dirLight.castShadow = true; heroMesh.castShadow = true; // renderer.shadowMap.enabled never set — shadows silently do not render

name: threejs: Selective Shadow Casting
guidance: Shadow map rendering redraws the entire scene from the light's perspective every frame. Enable castShadow only on the primary directional light and receiveShadow only on hero meshes and the ground plane.. Do: Enable shadows only on the key light and the most important meshes. Avoid: Enable castShadow and receiveShadow on every object in the scene including particles. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.shadowMap

details.Description: Shadow map rendering redraws the entire scene from the light's perspective every frame. Enable castShadow only on the primary directional light and receiveShadow only on hero meshes and the ground plane.
details.Do: Enable shadows only on the key light and the most important meshes
details.Don't: Enable castShadow and receiveShadow on every object in the scene including particles
details.Code Good: renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true; renderer.shadowMap.type = THREE.PCFSoftShadowMap; dirLight.castShadow = true; heroMesh.castShadow = true; ground.receiveShadow = true; // particles and background meshes: no shadow flags
details.Code Bad: for (const m of allMeshes) { m.castShadow = true; m.receiveShadow = true; } // shadow map pass over particle system — expensive with no visible gain

name: threejs: Skip Lights for MeshBasicMaterial
guidance: MeshBasicMaterial completely ignores all scene lights. Adding lights solely to illuminate BasicMaterial objects wastes a light pass on every frame with zero visible effect.. Do: Omit lights entirely when every material in the scene is MeshBasicMaterial. Avoid: Add AmbientLight and DirectionalLight to a scene that uses only MeshBasicMaterial. Severity: Low. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/materials/MeshBasicMaterial

details.Description: MeshBasicMaterial completely ignores all scene lights. Adding lights solely to illuminate BasicMaterial objects wastes a light pass on every frame with zero visible effect.
details.Do: Omit lights entirely when every material in the scene is MeshBasicMaterial
details.Don't: Add AmbientLight and DirectionalLight to a scene that uses only MeshBasicMaterial
details.Code Good: // Scene uses only MeshBasicMaterial — no lights needed const mat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0x00ffff }); const mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat); scene.add(mesh); // MeshBasicMaterial is always fully lit by definition
details.Code Bad: scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 1.0)); // wasted per-frame light pass — BasicMaterial ignores it entirely

name: threejs: Single Shared Raycaster
guidance: Create exactly one Raycaster instance outside all event handlers. Store mouse coordinates in pointermove (cheap). Call setFromCamera and intersectObjects together inside the animate() loop — once per frame instead of once per mouse event.. Do: Create one Raycaster; store mouse in pointermove; call setFromCamera + intersectObjects inside animate(). Avoid: Create a new THREE.Raycaster() inside a mousemove handler or call setFromCamera inside the event listener. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/Raycaster

details.Description: Create exactly one Raycaster instance outside all event handlers. Store mouse coordinates in pointermove (cheap). Call setFromCamera and intersectObjects together inside the animate() loop — once per frame instead of once per mouse event.
details.Do: Create one Raycaster; store mouse in pointermove; call setFromCamera + intersectObjects inside animate()
details.Don't: Create a new THREE.Raycaster() inside a mousemove handler or call setFromCamera inside the event listener
details.Code Good: const raycaster = new THREE.Raycaster(); const mouse = new THREE.Vector2(); canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', e => { // only store coords — no raycasting here mouse.x = (e.clientX / canvas.clientWidth) * 2 - 1; mouse.y = -(e.clientY / canvas.clientHeight) * 2 + 1; }); // setFromCamera and intersectObjects run once per frame in animate()
details.Code Bad: window.addEventListener('mousemove', e => { const rc = new THREE.Raycaster(); // new allocation per event rc.setFromCamera(mouse, camera); rc.intersectObjects(targets, true); // fires 200+ times/sec });

name: threejs: NDC Mouse Coordinates
guidance: Raycasting requires mouse in Normalized Device Coordinates: X from -1 (left) to +1 (right) and Y from +1 (top) to -1 (bottom). The Y axis is inverted relative to screen space. A missing negation on Y causes all raycasts to miss or hit the wrong objects.. Do: Apply the full NDC formula — including the negation on the Y axis. Avoid: Forget to negate Y — raycasting appears to work but hits objects mirrored vertically. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/Raycaster.setFromCamera

details.Description: Raycasting requires mouse in Normalized Device Coordinates: X from -1 (left) to +1 (right) and Y from +1 (top) to -1 (bottom). The Y axis is inverted relative to screen space. A missing negation on Y causes all raycasts to miss or hit the wrong objects.
details.Do: Apply the full NDC formula — including the negation on the Y axis
details.Don't: Forget to negate Y — raycasting appears to work but hits objects mirrored vertically
details.Code Good: mouse.x = (e.clientX / canvas.clientWidth) * 2 - 1; mouse.y = -(e.clientY / canvas.clientHeight) * 2 + 1; // Y is INVERTED
details.Code Bad: mouse.x = (e.clientX / canvas.clientWidth) * 2 - 1; mouse.y = (e.clientY / canvas.clientHeight) * 2 - 1; // BUG: Y not negated — raycasting is mirrored

name: threejs: setFromCamera and intersectObjects in animate
guidance: Call raycaster.setFromCamera(mouse camera) and then raycaster.intersectObjects(targets true) inside the animate() loop. setFromCamera must come before intersectObjects every frame — without it the raycaster uses a stale ray direction.. Do: Call setFromCamera then intersectObjects in order inside every animate() frame. Avoid: Call intersectObjects without calling setFromCamera first — the raycaster uses a stale or zero ray. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/Raycaster

details.Description: Call raycaster.setFromCamera(mouse camera) and then raycaster.intersectObjects(targets true) inside the animate() loop. setFromCamera must come before intersectObjects every frame — without it the raycaster uses a stale ray direction.
details.Do: Call setFromCamera then intersectObjects in order inside every animate() frame
details.Don't: Call intersectObjects without calling setFromCamera first — the raycaster uses a stale or zero ray
details.Code Good: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); raycaster.setFromCamera(mouse, camera); // update ray direction first const hits = raycaster.intersectObjects(targets, true); // then test intersections if (hits.length > 0) { document.body.style.cursor = 'pointer'; } else { document.body.style.cursor = 'auto'; } renderer.render(scene, camera); }
details.Code Bad: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); const hits = raycaster.intersectObjects(targets, true); // BUG: setFromCamera never called — stale ray — hits is always empty renderer.render(scene, camera); }

name: threejs: Recursive Flag for Groups and GLTF
guidance: Pass true as the second argument to intersectObjects when testing Groups or GLTF loaded models. Geometry lives on child Mesh objects — without recursive:true the parent group is tested but has no geometry and hits is always empty.. Do: Use intersectObjects(targets true) for Groups or any loaded model. Avoid: Raycast against a parent Group without the recursive flag. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/Raycaster.intersectObjects

details.Guideline: Recursive Flag for Groups and GLTF
details.Description: Pass true as the second argument to intersectObjects when testing Groups or GLTF loaded models. Geometry lives on child Mesh objects — without recursive:true the parent group is tested but has no geometry and hits is always empty.
details.Do: Use intersectObjects(targets true) for Groups or any loaded model
details.Don't: Raycast against a parent Group without the recursive flag
details.Code Good: const hits = raycaster.intersectObjects(scene.children, true); // catches all descendant meshes
details.Code Bad: const hits = raycaster.intersectObjects([modelGroup]); // recursive defaults to false — misses all children

name: threejs: Cursor Feedback on Hover
guidance: Set document.body.style.cursor = 'pointer' when intersections are found and reset to 'auto' when none are found. Without cursor feedback users cannot discover that 3D objects are interactive.. Do: Update cursor to pointer on hit; reset to auto on miss in the same animate loop block. Avoid: Run raycasting and read hits without ever updating the cursor style. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/cursor

details.Description: Set document.body.style.cursor = 'pointer' when intersections are found and reset to 'auto' when none are found. Without cursor feedback users cannot discover that 3D objects are interactive.
details.Do: Update cursor to pointer on hit; reset to auto on miss in the same animate loop block
details.Don't: Run raycasting and read hits without ever updating the cursor style
details.Code Bad: raycaster.setFromCamera(mouse, camera); raycaster.intersectObjects(targets, true); // hits ignored — cursor never changes — objects feel non-interactive

name: threejs: requestAnimationFrame Loop Only
guidance: Drive the render loop exclusively with requestAnimationFrame or renderer.setAnimationLoop(). Never use setInterval or setTimeout — they are not synchronized to the display refresh rate and keep running when the tab is hidden draining battery.. Do: Use requestAnimationFrame or renderer.setAnimationLoop() as the sole render loop driver. Avoid: Use setInterval or setTimeout for render timing. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.setAnimationLoop

details.Description: Drive the render loop exclusively with requestAnimationFrame or renderer.setAnimationLoop(). Never use setInterval or setTimeout — they are not synchronized to the display refresh rate and keep running when the tab is hidden draining battery.
details.Do: Use requestAnimationFrame or renderer.setAnimationLoop() as the sole render loop driver
details.Don't: Use setInterval or setTimeout for render timing
details.Code Good: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); renderer.render(scene, camera); } animate();
details.Code Bad: setInterval(() => renderer.render(scene, camera), 16); // not display-synced; runs at full speed even when tab is hidden

name: threejs: THREE.Clock for Delta Time
guidance: Use THREE.Clock and clock.getDelta() for all time-based motion. A hardcoded increment like += 0.01 runs at 2x speed on 120Hz displays and at unpredictable speed when frames drop under load. CRITICAL: call getDelta() exactly ONCE per animate() frame and store the result in a local dt variable. getDelta() resets the internal clock on every call — a second call in the same frame always returns ~0, silently breaking any animation block that uses it.. Do: Call clock.getDelta() once at the top of animate(); store result in dt; reuse dt everywhere in that frame. Avoid: Call clock.getDelta() more than once per frame or inside a helper called from animate(). Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/Clock

details.Description: Use THREE.Clock and clock.getDelta() for all time-based motion. A hardcoded increment like += 0.01 runs at 2x speed on 120Hz displays and at unpredictable speed when frames drop under load. CRITICAL: call getDelta() exactly ONCE per animate() frame and store the result in a local dt variable. getDelta() resets the internal clock on every call — a second call in the same frame always returns ~0, silently breaking any animation block that uses it.
details.Do: Call clock.getDelta() once at the top of animate(); store result in dt; reuse dt everywhere in that frame
details.Don't: Call clock.getDelta() more than once per frame or inside a helper called from animate()
details.Code Good: const clock = new THREE.Clock(); function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); const dt = clock.getDelta(); // called ONCE — reuse dt below mesh.rotation.y += dt * 0.8; particles.rotation.y += dt * 0.1; // reuse dt, not a second getDelta() renderer.render(scene, camera); }
details.Code Bad: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); mesh.rotation.y += 0.01; // 0.01 rad/frame — runs 2x faster on 120Hz than on 60Hz }

name: threejs: Lerp for Smooth Pointer Follow
guidance: Use value += (target - value) * alpha each frame to smoothly interpolate toward a moving target. An alpha of 0.03–0.1 produces organic easing for camera follow pointer-tracking and hover scale effects without requiring GSAP.. Do: Apply the lerp formula each frame with a small alpha for smooth organic motion. Avoid: Snap a value directly to the target producing an instant jarring jump. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/math/MathUtils.lerp

details.Description: Use value += (target - value) * alpha each frame to smoothly interpolate toward a moving target. An alpha of 0.03–0.1 produces organic easing for camera follow pointer-tracking and hover scale effects without requiring GSAP.
details.Do: Apply the lerp formula each frame with a small alpha for smooth organic motion
details.Don't: Snap a value directly to the target producing an instant jarring jump
details.Code Good: // In animate(): cameraTargetX = mouse.x * 3; camera.position.x += (cameraTargetX - camera.position.x) * 0.05; camera.position.y += (cameraTargetY - camera.position.y) * 0.05; camera.lookAt(scene.position);
details.Code Bad: // In animate(): camera.position.x = mouse.x * 3; // instant snap — jarring with no easing

name: threejs: GSAP for Multi-Step Sequences
guidance: Use GSAP timelines for any animation with more than two sequential steps or for scroll-linked camera paths. GSAP timelines can be paused reversed and scrubbed — far more maintainable than boolean state machines.. Do: Use GSAP timelines for sequences with more than two steps and for scroll-driven animations. Avoid: Implement multi-step sequences with boolean flags and manual frame counters. Severity: High. Docs: https://gsap.com/docs/v3/GSAP/Timeline/

details.Description: Use GSAP timelines for any animation with more than two sequential steps or for scroll-linked camera paths. GSAP timelines can be paused reversed and scrubbed — far more maintainable than boolean state machines.
details.Do: Use GSAP timelines for sequences with more than two steps and for scroll-driven animations
details.Don't: Implement multi-step sequences with boolean flags and manual frame counters
details.Code Good: const tl = gsap.timeline({ defaults: { ease: 'power2.out' } }); tl.from(mesh.position, { y: -3, duration: 1 }) .to(mesh.rotation, { y: Math.PI, duration: 1 }, '-=0.3') .to(camera.position, { z: 2, duration: 1.5 });
details.Code Bad: let step = 0; let t = 0; function animate() { if (step === 0 && (t += 0.01) >= 1) step = 1; } // grows unmanageable with 3+ steps

name: threejs: Pause Render Loop on Tab Hidden
guidance: Use renderer.setAnimationLoop() as the loop driver so you can pass null to pause and a function to resume. Continuous rendering in a hidden tab wastes CPU GPU and battery with no user benefit.. Do: Use renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate) to drive the loop; pass null to pause on visibilitychange. Avoid: Drive with internal requestAnimationFrame and never stop the loop when the tab is hidden. Severity: High. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Page_Visibility_API

details.Guideline: Pause Render Loop on Tab Hidden
details.Description: Use renderer.setAnimationLoop() as the loop driver so you can pass null to pause and a function to resume. Continuous rendering in a hidden tab wastes CPU GPU and battery with no user benefit.
details.Do: Use renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate) to drive the loop; pass null to pause on visibilitychange
details.Don't: Drive with internal requestAnimationFrame and never stop the loop when the tab is hidden
details.Code Good: renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate); // use setAnimationLoop as the driver — not requestAnimationFrame inside animate function animate() { const dt = clock.getDelta(); renderer.render(scene, camera); } document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => { if (document.hidden) renderer.setAnimationLoop(null); else renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate); });
details.Code Bad: function animate() { requestAnimationFrame(animate); // self-referencing RAF cannot be stopped externally renderer.render(scene, camera); } animate(); // runs forever in background tab — drains battery

name: threejs: Load GSAP Before Scene Script
guidance: Load GSAP from its own CDN script tag before your scene script. In bundler projects install via npm and import. GSAP is a completely separate library from Three.js — never try to import it from the Three.js package.. Do: Load GSAP CDN before the scene script; or npm install gsap and import separately. Avoid: Import gsap from three or expect it to be defined without a separate load. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://gsap.com/docs/v3/Installation

details.Description: Load GSAP from its own CDN script tag before your scene script. In bundler projects install via npm and import. GSAP is a completely separate library from Three.js — never try to import it from the Three.js package.
details.Do: Load GSAP CDN before the scene script; or npm install gsap and import separately
details.Don't: Import gsap from three or expect it to be defined without a separate load
details.Code Good: <!-- CDN: load GSAP before your scene script --> <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.2/gsap.min.js"></script> <!-- Bundler: --> // import gsap from 'gsap'; import { ScrollTrigger } from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger';
details.Code Bad: import gsap from 'three'; // undefined — GSAP has nothing to do with Three.js

name: threejs: Register ScrollTrigger Before Use
guidance: Call gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) once at the top of your script before any scrollTrigger config object. Without registration the ScrollTrigger name is undefined and the tween throws immediately.. Do: Call gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) as the first line before any gsap.to/from/timeline with scrollTrigger. Avoid: Include scrollTrigger config in gsap.to() calls without first registering the plugin. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://gsap.com/docs/v3/Plugins/ScrollTrigger/

details.Description: Call gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) once at the top of your script before any scrollTrigger config object. Without registration the ScrollTrigger name is undefined and the tween throws immediately.
details.Do: Call gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) as the first line before any gsap.to/from/timeline with scrollTrigger
details.Don't: Include scrollTrigger config in gsap.to() calls without first registering the plugin
details.Code Good: gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger); gsap.to(camera.position, { z: 2, scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.hero-section', scrub: 1 } });
details.Code Bad: gsap.to(mesh.position, { scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.section', scrub: true } }); // TypeError: ScrollTrigger is not a constructor — not registered

name: threejs: Tween Three.js Properties Directly
guidance: GSAP can tween any numeric JavaScript property including mesh.position.x mesh.rotation.y and material.opacity. No wrapper or adaptor is needed. Note: to tween material.opacity the material must have transparent:true set before the tween starts.. Do: Pass Three.js object properties directly to gsap.to(); set transparent:true before tweening opacity. Avoid: Use a plain proxy object then manually copy values to Three.js properties every frame. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://gsap.com/docs/v3/GSAP/gsap.to()

details.Description: GSAP can tween any numeric JavaScript property including mesh.position.x mesh.rotation.y and material.opacity. No wrapper or adaptor is needed. Note: to tween material.opacity the material must have transparent:true set before the tween starts.
details.Do: Pass Three.js object properties directly to gsap.to(); set transparent:true before tweening opacity
details.Don't: Use a plain proxy object then manually copy values to Three.js properties every frame
details.Code Good: gsap.to(mesh.rotation, { y: Math.PI * 2, duration: 2, ease: 'power1.inOut' }); mesh.material.transparent = true; // required before tweening opacity gsap.to(mesh.material, { opacity: 0, duration: 1 });
details.Code Bad: const tw = { v: 0 }; gsap.to(tw, { v: Math.PI * 2, onUpdate: () => mesh.rotation.y = tw.v }); // unnecessary proxy wrapper

name: threejs: scrub for Scroll-Driven Camera Path
guidance: Use scrub:true or scrub:1 to link camera movement continuously to scroll position as a 0–1 ratio. scrub:1 adds a 1-second lag for cinematic smoothness. onEnter/onLeave fire only once and create jarring snaps — not the right tool for a camera path.. Do: Use scrub:1 for any scroll-controlled camera movement. Avoid: Use onEnter or onLeave callbacks for camera motion — they snap instead of scrubbing. Severity: High. Docs: https://gsap.com/docs/v3/Plugins/ScrollTrigger/

details.Description: Use scrub:true or scrub:1 to link camera movement continuously to scroll position as a 0–1 ratio. scrub:1 adds a 1-second lag for cinematic smoothness. onEnter/onLeave fire only once and create jarring snaps — not the right tool for a camera path.
details.Do: Use scrub:1 for any scroll-controlled camera movement
details.Don't: Use onEnter or onLeave callbacks for camera motion — they snap instead of scrubbing
details.Code Good: gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger); gsap.to(camera.position, { x: 5, y: 2, z: 0, ease: 'none', scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.canvas-wrapper', start: 'top top', end: 'bottom bottom', scrub: 1 } });
details.Code Bad: gsap.to(camera.position, { z: 0, scrollTrigger: { trigger: '.section', onEnter: () => {} } }); // fires once at scroll threshold — not a continuous scrub

name: threejs: InstancedMesh for Repeated Objects
guidance: Use THREE.InstancedMesh when rendering 50 or more identical objects. It submits all N transforms in one draw call instead of N draw calls and reduces CPU-GPU communication overhead dramatically.. Do: Use InstancedMesh for any group of 50+ meshes sharing the same geometry and material. Avoid: Create 50+ separate Mesh objects with the same geometry and material. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/objects/InstancedMesh

details.Description: Use THREE.InstancedMesh when rendering 50 or more identical objects. It submits all N transforms in one draw call instead of N draw calls and reduces CPU-GPU communication overhead dramatically.
details.Do: Use InstancedMesh for any group of 50+ meshes sharing the same geometry and material
details.Don't: Create 50+ separate Mesh objects with the same geometry and material
details.Code Good: const COUNT = 500; const iMesh = new THREE.InstancedMesh(geo, mat, COUNT); const matrix = new THREE.Matrix4(); for (let i = 0; i < COUNT; i++) { matrix.setPosition(Math.random()*10, Math.random()*10, Math.random()*10); iMesh.setMatrixAt(i, matrix); } iMesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true; scene.add(iMesh);
details.Code Bad: for (let i = 0; i < 500; i++) { scene.add(new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat)); } // 500 separate draw calls per frame

name: threejs: Tone Mapping and sRGB Encoding
guidance: Enable ACESFilmicToneMapping and sRGBEncoding on the renderer for accurate perceptual color. Without tone mapping colors appear washed out or over-saturated. These are renderer properties set after construction and take effect immediately.. Do: Set renderer.toneMapping and renderer.outputEncoding after construction for all production scenes. Avoid: Leave tone mapping and output encoding at defaults when the scene targets realistic visuals. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.toneMapping

details.Description: Enable ACESFilmicToneMapping and sRGBEncoding on the renderer for accurate perceptual color. Without tone mapping colors appear washed out or over-saturated. These are renderer properties set after construction and take effect immediately.
details.Do: Set renderer.toneMapping and renderer.outputEncoding after construction for all production scenes
details.Don't: Leave tone mapping and output encoding at defaults when the scene targets realistic visuals
details.Code Good: renderer.toneMapping = THREE.ACESFilmicToneMapping; renderer.toneMappingExposure = 1.0; renderer.outputEncoding = THREE.sRGBEncoding; // correct for r128
details.Code Bad: const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer(); // defaults: NoToneMapping + LinearEncoding — colors appear flat and incorrect

name: threejs: antialias Set at Construction Only
guidance: The antialias option can only be set at WebGLRenderer construction time. Setting renderer.antialias after construction has absolutely no effect — the WebGL context is already created without it. Decide before instantiating.. Do: Set antialias:true inside the WebGLRenderer constructor options object. Avoid: Construct the renderer without antialias then try to enable it by assigning the property. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer

details.Description: The antialias option can only be set at WebGLRenderer construction time. Setting renderer.antialias after construction has absolutely no effect — the WebGL context is already created without it. Decide before instantiating.
details.Do: Set antialias:true inside the WebGLRenderer constructor options object
details.Don't: Construct the renderer without antialias then try to enable it by assigning the property
details.Code Good: const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ antialias: true, alpha: true }); // antialias baked into the WebGL context
details.Code Bad: const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer(); renderer.antialias = true; // no effect — context created without AA — edges remain aliased

name: threejs: FogExp2 for Depth and Far Culling
guidance: Use scene.fog to create atmospheric depth. As a secondary benefit objects that disappear into fog before the far plane stop contributing to draw calls — useful in scenes with large view distances.. Do: Add FogExp2 to scenes with view distances above 100 units for both visual atmosphere and implicit far culling. Avoid: Ignore fog in scenes with far:1000+ and many distant objects that contribute tiny pixels per draw call. Severity: Low. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/scenes/FogExp2

details.Guideline: FogExp2 for Depth and Far Culling
details.Description: Use scene.fog to create atmospheric depth. As a secondary benefit objects that disappear into fog before the far plane stop contributing to draw calls — useful in scenes with large view distances.
details.Do: Add FogExp2 to scenes with view distances above 100 units for both visual atmosphere and implicit far culling
details.Don't: Ignore fog in scenes with far:1000+ and many distant objects that contribute tiny pixels per draw call
details.Code Good: scene.fog = new THREE.FogExp2(0x0a0a0a, 0.02); // exponential — density feels more natural than linear
details.Code Bad: // far: 2000 with no fog — hundreds of distant objects too small to see still cost draw calls per frame

name: threejs: BufferGeometry Plus Points for Particle Systems
guidance: Build all particle systems with BufferGeometry plus a Float32Array position attribute rendered as Points. Never use individual Mesh objects as particles — they cannot scale past a few hundred with good performance.. Do: Use Points plus BufferGeometry for all particle effects. Avoid: Create hundreds of individual Mesh objects to simulate a particle system. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/objects/Points

details.Guideline: BufferGeometry Plus Points for Particle Systems
details.Description: Build all particle systems with BufferGeometry plus a Float32Array position attribute rendered as Points. Never use individual Mesh objects as particles — they cannot scale past a few hundred with good performance.
details.Do: Use Points plus BufferGeometry for all particle effects
details.Don't: Create hundreds of individual Mesh objects to simulate a particle system
details.Code Good: const COUNT = 3000; const geo = new THREE.BufferGeometry(); const pos = new Float32Array(COUNT * 3); for (let i = 0; i < COUNT * 3; i++) pos[i] = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 20; geo.setAttribute('position', new THREE.BufferAttribute(pos, 3)); const particles = new THREE.Points(geo, new THREE.PointsMaterial({ size: 0.05, color: 0xffffff })); scene.add(particles);
details.Code Bad: for (let i = 0; i < 500; i++) { scene.add(new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.SphereGeometry(0.05, 8, 8), mat)); } // 500 separate draw calls per frame

name: threejs: Particle Count Ceiling
guidance: Start particle systems at 1000–3000 particles. Beyond 50000 causes sustained frame drops on mid-range mobile. Always test on a real device before increasing the count — desktop and mobile GPU performance ratios can be 10:1.. Do: Start at 3000 particles and profile on actual mobile hardware before raising the limit. Avoid: Set particle count at 100000 or higher without any mobile profiling. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/objects/Points

details.Description: Start particle systems at 1000–3000 particles. Beyond 50000 causes sustained frame drops on mid-range mobile. Always test on a real device before increasing the count — desktop and mobile GPU performance ratios can be 10:1.
details.Do: Start at 3000 particles and profile on actual mobile hardware before raising the limit
details.Don't: Set particle count at 100000 or higher without any mobile profiling
details.Code Good: const COUNT = 3000; // safe mobile baseline — profile before going higher const pos = new Float32Array(COUNT * 3);
details.Code Bad: const COUNT = 150000; // 60fps on desktop — 8fps on a mid-range Android phone

name: threejs: needsUpdate After Buffer Mutation
guidance: After mutating any BufferAttribute array values per frame you must set geometry.attributes.position.needsUpdate = true so Three.js re-uploads the changed buffer to the GPU. Without it the GPU still uses the old data and particles appear completely frozen.. Do: Set needsUpdate = true on the position attribute after every per-frame mutation of the array. Avoid: Mutate the Float32Array values without flagging needsUpdate — positions update in JS but not on the GPU. Severity: Critical. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/core/BufferAttribute.needsUpdate

details.Description: After mutating any BufferAttribute array values per frame you must set geometry.attributes.position.needsUpdate = true so Three.js re-uploads the changed buffer to the GPU. Without it the GPU still uses the old data and particles appear completely frozen.
details.Do: Set needsUpdate = true on the position attribute after every per-frame mutation of the array
details.Don't: Mutate the Float32Array values without flagging needsUpdate — positions update in JS but not on the GPU
details.Code Good: // In animate(): const pos = geo.attributes.position.array; for (let i = 0; i < pos.length; i += 3) { pos[i + 1] += Math.sin(clock.getElapsedTime() + i) * 0.001; // Y component } geo.attributes.position.needsUpdate = true; // GPU re-upload
details.Code Bad: // In animate(): pos[1] += 0.001; // JS array updated — GPU buffer is stale — particles do not move

name: threejs: Canvas Dimensions Not Window
guidance: Size the renderer and camera to the canvas element's clientWidth and clientHeight — not window.innerWidth and innerHeight. This is correct when the canvas is inside a flex or grid container that does not fill the full viewport.. Do: Use canvas.clientWidth and canvas.clientHeight for all renderer and camera sizing. Avoid: Hardcode renderer size to window.innerWidth/innerHeight when the canvas may be inside a container. Severity: High. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/renderers/WebGLRenderer.setSize

details.Description: Size the renderer and camera to the canvas element's clientWidth and clientHeight — not window.innerWidth and innerHeight. This is correct when the canvas is inside a flex or grid container that does not fill the full viewport.
details.Do: Use canvas.clientWidth and canvas.clientHeight for all renderer and camera sizing
details.Don't: Hardcode renderer size to window.innerWidth/innerHeight when the canvas may be inside a container
details.Code Good: renderer.setSize(canvas.clientWidth, canvas.clientHeight); camera.aspect = canvas.clientWidth / canvas.clientHeight; camera.updateProjectionMatrix();
details.Code Bad: renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight); // wrong when canvas lives inside a sidebar or grid column

name: threejs: ResizeObserver Over window resize Event
guidance: Use ResizeObserver on the canvas container instead of the window resize event. ResizeObserver fires when the container element changes size independently of the browser window — common in split-pane layouts and sidebar collapsing.. Do: Attach ResizeObserver to the canvas parent element for accurate container-aware resize detection. Avoid: Use only window.addEventListener('resize') for canvas sizing when the canvas is not fullscreen. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver

details.Description: Use ResizeObserver on the canvas container instead of the window resize event. ResizeObserver fires when the container element changes size independently of the browser window — common in split-pane layouts and sidebar collapsing.
details.Do: Attach ResizeObserver to the canvas parent element for accurate container-aware resize detection
details.Don't: Use only window.addEventListener('resize') for canvas sizing when the canvas is not fullscreen
details.Code Good: const ro = new ResizeObserver(entries => { const { width, height } = entries[0].contentRect; renderer.setSize(width, height); camera.aspect = width / height; camera.updateProjectionMatrix(); }); ro.observe(canvas.parentElement);
details.Code Bad: window.addEventListener('resize', () => { renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight); }); // misses container-only resize events in split-pane UIs

name: threejs: Touch Events for Mobile Interaction
guidance: Add touchstart and touchmove listeners alongside mouse events so the scene remains interactive on mobile. Normalize touch coordinates to the same NDC range as mouse events and pass passive:false on touchmove if you call preventDefault.. Do: Handle both mouse and touch input for any interactive 3D scene. Avoid: Add only mouse event listeners and leave touch users with no interaction. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Touch_events

details.Description: Add touchstart and touchmove listeners alongside mouse events so the scene remains interactive on mobile. Normalize touch coordinates to the same NDC range as mouse events and pass passive:false on touchmove if you call preventDefault.
details.Do: Handle both mouse and touch input for any interactive 3D scene
details.Don't: Add only mouse event listeners and leave touch users with no interaction
details.Code Good: canvas.addEventListener('touchmove', e => { e.preventDefault(); const t = e.touches[0]; mouse.x = (t.clientX / canvas.clientWidth) * 2 - 1; mouse.y = -(t.clientY / canvas.clientHeight) * 2 + 1; }, { passive: false }); canvas.addEventListener('touchstart', e => { e.preventDefault(); }, { passive: false });
details.Code Bad: canvas.addEventListener('mousemove', handleMouse); // touch events unhandled — mobile users get no interaction

name: threejs: prefers-reduced-motion
guidance: Check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') before starting any auto-rotation, particle animation, or camera movement. Users who enable this OS preference have motion sickness or vestibular disorders. IMPORTANT: reading .matches once at page load is a one-time snapshot — if the user changes their OS accessibility setting mid-session the scene will not react. Attach a 'change' listener to the MediaQueryList so noMotion stays in sync at runtime.. Do: Use matchMedia.addEventListener('change') to keep noMotion reactive; gate all auto-animation on the live value. Avoid: Read .matches once at startup and never update it — the scene ignores mid-session OS setting changes. Severity: High. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/prefers-reduced-motion

details.Description: Check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') before starting any auto-rotation, particle animation, or camera movement. Users who enable this OS preference have motion sickness or vestibular disorders. IMPORTANT: reading .matches once at page load is a one-time snapshot — if the user changes their OS accessibility setting mid-session the scene will not react. Attach a 'change' listener to the MediaQueryList so noMotion stays in sync at runtime.
details.Do: Use matchMedia.addEventListener('change') to keep noMotion reactive; gate all auto-animation on the live value
details.Don't: Read .matches once at startup and never update it — the scene ignores mid-session OS setting changes
details.Code Good: const mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'); let noMotion = mq.matches; mq.addEventListener('change', e => { noMotion = e.matches; }); // In animate(): if (!noMotion) { mesh.rotation.y += dt * 0.8; particles.rotation.y += dt * 0.1; }
details.Code Bad: const noMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches; // one-time snapshot — mid-session OS change is ignored entirely

name: threejs: Canvas aria-label
guidance: Add role='img' and a descriptive aria-label to renderer.domElement after appending it to the DOM. Screen readers receive no information from a WebGL canvas — the aria-label is the only description they can announce to users.. Do: Set role='img' and a meaningful aria-label on renderer.domElement before or after appending it. Avoid: Append the canvas to the DOM with no accessibility attributes — invisible to screen readers. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/canvas#accessibility_concerns

details.Description: Add role='img' and a descriptive aria-label to renderer.domElement after appending it to the DOM. Screen readers receive no information from a WebGL canvas — the aria-label is the only description they can announce to users.
details.Do: Set role='img' and a meaningful aria-label on renderer.domElement before or after appending it
details.Don't: Append the canvas to the DOM with no accessibility attributes — invisible to screen readers
details.Code Good: renderer.domElement.setAttribute('role', 'img'); renderer.domElement.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Interactive 3D product viewer. Drag to rotate. Scroll to zoom.'); document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);
details.Code Bad: document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement); // bare canvas — screen readers announce nothing

name: threejs: Bundler Stack for Production
guidance: For production use Three.js via npm plus Vite. You get full tree-shaking reduced bundle size access to the complete examples/jsm library including OrbitControls GLTFLoader and EffectComposer and TypeScript support.. Do: Use npm install three plus Vite or Webpack for any production client-facing project. Avoid: Serve raw CDN script tags in a production application that needs tree-shaking or TypeScript. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#manual/en/introduction/Installation

details.Description: For production use Three.js via npm plus Vite. You get full tree-shaking reduced bundle size access to the complete examples/jsm library including OrbitControls GLTFLoader and EffectComposer and TypeScript support.
details.Do: Use npm install three plus Vite or Webpack for any production client-facing project
details.Don't: Serve raw CDN script tags in a production application that needs tree-shaking or TypeScript
details.Code Good: npm install three gsap // then in your JS: import * as THREE from 'three'; import { OrbitControls } from 'three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js'; import { GLTFLoader } from 'three/examples/jsm/loaders/GLTFLoader.js';
details.Code Bad: <!-- In a Vite/React production build: --> <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/three.min.js"></script> // no tree-shaking — entire Three.js ships

name: threejs: GLTFLoader with scene traverse
guidance: Load 3D models using GLTFLoader and traverse gltf.scene to configure castShadow receiveShadow and material overrides on all child Mesh nodes. Calling scene.add(gltf.scene) alone silently skips all shadow and material configuration.. Do: Use GLTFLoader and traverse the entire gltf.scene graph to set up shadows and materials on every Mesh child. Avoid: Load a GLTF model and pass gltf.scene directly to scene.add without traversing child meshes. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#examples/en/loaders/GLTFLoader

details.Description: Load 3D models using GLTFLoader and traverse gltf.scene to configure castShadow receiveShadow and material overrides on all child Mesh nodes. Calling scene.add(gltf.scene) alone silently skips all shadow and material configuration.
details.Do: Use GLTFLoader and traverse the entire gltf.scene graph to set up shadows and materials on every Mesh child
details.Don't: Load a GLTF model and pass gltf.scene directly to scene.add without traversing child meshes
details.Code Good: import { GLTFLoader } from 'three/examples/jsm/loaders/GLTFLoader.js'; const loader = new GLTFLoader(); loader.load('model.glb', gltf => { gltf.scene.traverse(child => { if (child.isMesh) { child.castShadow = true; child.receiveShadow = true; } }); scene.add(gltf.scene); });
details.Code Bad: loader.load('model.glb', gltf => { scene.add(gltf.scene); // shadows and material setup silently skipped on all children });

name: threejs: LOD for Distance-Based Detail
guidance: Use THREE.LOD to automatically swap high-detail and low-detail geometry as objects move closer or farther from the camera. This maintains frame rate in scenes with many objects spread across a large depth range.. Do: Use THREE.LOD to reduce triangle count on distant objects automatically. Avoid: Render the same high-polygon geometry for every object regardless of its distance from the camera. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://threejs.org/docs/#api/en/objects/LOD

details.Description: Use THREE.LOD to automatically swap high-detail and low-detail geometry as objects move closer or farther from the camera. This maintains frame rate in scenes with many objects spread across a large depth range.
details.Do: Use THREE.LOD to reduce triangle count on distant objects automatically
details.Don't: Render the same high-polygon geometry for every object regardless of its distance from the camera
details.Code Good: const lod = new THREE.LOD(); lod.addLevel(highDetailMesh, 0); // used when < 15 units away lod.addLevel(medDetailMesh, 15); // 15–50 units lod.addLevel(lowDetailMesh, 50); // 50+ units scene.add(lod);
details.Code Bad: scene.add(highDetailMesh); // 64k-triangle mesh rendered at full cost whether 1 unit or 100 units from camera

name: uno: Show an extended splash screen on WASM
guidance: WASM bundle download and runtime startup take several seconds on first load — render branded UI immediately so users do not see a blank page (AOT and trimming are covered separately). Do: Render a splash overlay in wwwroot/index.html that hides on first XAML navigation. Avoid: Letting the user wait on a blank white page while the runtime boots. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/external/uno.wasm.bootstrap/doc/runtime-execution-modes.html

details.Guideline: Show an extended splash screen on WASM
details.Description: WASM bundle download and runtime startup take several seconds on first load — render branded UI immediately so users do not see a blank page (AOT and trimming are covered separately)
details.Do: Render a splash overlay in wwwroot/index.html that hides on first XAML navigation
details.Don't: Letting the user wait on a blank white page while the runtime boots
details.Code Good:
index.html:
Loading…
hidden via JS interop after first Frame.Navigate
details.Code Bad: No splash markup in index.html — 5-second blank page on first visit

name: uno: Use AOT compilation for performance
guidance: Ahead-of-time compilation improves runtime speed. Do: AOT for production WASM builds. Avoid: Interpreter mode in production. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/external/uno.wasm.bootstrap/doc/runtime-execution-modes.html

details.Do: NavigationView with MenuItems for app navigation

guidance: Cross-platform modal dialogs using WinUI API. Do: ContentDialog for confirmations and input. Avoid: Custom overlay Panel as dialog. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/controls/dialogs-and-flyouts/dialogs

guidance: Standard command bar with primary and secondary commands. Do: CommandBar with AppBarButtons for toolbar actions. Avoid: Custom StackPanel toolbar. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/controls/command-bar

guidance: Platform-native toggle control for on/off preferences. Do: ToggleSwitch for settings and feature flags. Avoid: CheckBox for toggle settings. Severity: Low. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/controls/toggles

name: uno: Implement INotifyPropertyChanged
guidance: Enable UI updates when ViewModel properties change. Do: CommunityToolkit.Mvvm [ObservableProperty] for auto-notification. Avoid: Properties without change notification. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/communitytoolkit/mvvm/

details.Description: Enable UI updates when ViewModel properties change
details.Code Good: [ObservableProperty] public partial string Title { get; set; }
details.Code Bad: public string Title { get; set; } without notification

name: uno: Use ObservableCollection for bound lists
guidance: Collection change notifications for ItemsSources across platforms. Do: ObservableCollection<T> for data-bound lists. Avoid: List<T> for bound ItemsSources. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/communitytoolkit/mvvm/

details.Description: Collection change notifications for ItemsSources across platforms

name: uno: Handle app suspension on mobile
guidance: iOS and Android may suspend or terminate the app — WinAppSDK desktop does not raise Suspending so use window Closed for desktop save-state. Do: Save state in OnSuspending and restore on activation. Avoid: Ignoring lifecycle losing user state on mobile. Severity: High. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/windows-ui-xaml-application.html

details.Description: iOS and Android may suspend or terminate the app — WinAppSDK desktop does not raise Suspending so use window Closed for desktop save-state
details.Do: Save state in OnSuspending and restore on activation
details.Don't: Ignoring lifecycle losing user state on mobile
details.Code Good: Application.Current.Suspending += (s, e) => { var d = e.SuspendingOperation.GetDeferral(); SaveState(); d.Complete(); };
details.Code Bad: No suspend handler losing form data on mobile

name: uno: Use Uno.Extensions.Hosting for startup
guidance: Structured app initialization with DI and configuration. Do: IHost builder pattern for app startup and service registration. Avoid: Manual initialization in App constructor. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/external/uno.extensions/doc/Overview/Hosting/HostingOverview.html

details.Description: Structured app initialization with DI and configuration
details.Do: IHost builder pattern for app startup and service registration

name: uno: Use ListView virtualization for large lists
guidance: Only renders visible items to reduce memory and layout cost. Do: ListView with default ItemsStackPanel virtualization. Avoid: ItemsControl or StackPanel for large data sets. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/controls/listview-and-gridview

details.Guideline: Use ListView virtualization for large lists
details.Description: Only renders visible items to reduce memory and layout cost
details.Don't: ItemsControl or StackPanel for large data sets

name: uno: Support keyboard navigation on desktop
guidance: Skia and WinAppSDK targets need full keyboard operability — note TabIndex routing is not fully implemented on every Uno target. Do: AccessKey and KeyboardAccelerator on Skia and WinAppSDK targets. Avoid: Mouse-only interactions on desktop. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/input/keyboard-accelerators

details.Description: Skia and WinAppSDK targets need full keyboard operability — note TabIndex routing is not fully implemented on every Uno target

name: uno: Use service workers for offline support
guidance: Enable PWA capabilities for WASM deployments. Do: Service worker registration for caching and offline mode. Avoid: Online-only WASM app with no offline fallback. Severity: Low. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/external/uno.wasm.bootstrap/doc/features-pwa.html

details.Guideline: Use service workers for offline support
