name: uno: Marshal to UI thread with DispatcherQueue
guidance: Cross-thread access to UI elements throws — capture the UI DispatcherQueue once and use TryEnqueue to update from background work. Do: DispatcherQueue.GetForCurrentThread().TryEnqueue from background work. Avoid: Touching UI controls directly from a Task. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/windows-app-sdk/api/winrt/microsoft.ui.dispatching.dispatcherqueue

details.Guideline: Marshal to UI thread with DispatcherQueue
details.Description: Cross-thread access to UI elements throws — capture the UI DispatcherQueue once and use TryEnqueue to update from background work
details.Don't: Touching UI controls directly from a Task
details.Code Bad: await Task.Run(() => StatusText.Text = "Done"); throws on non-UI thread

name: uno: Merge XamlControlsResources in App.xaml
guidance: Required for Fluent control styles to load — without it controls render with no template. Do: Add XamlControlsResources at the top of Application.Resources MergedDictionaries. Avoid: Skipping the merged dictionary and wondering why Buttons look unstyled. Severity: High. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/fluent-styles.html

details.Description: Required for Fluent control styles to load — without it controls render with no template
details.Do: Add XamlControlsResources at the top of Application.Resources MergedDictionaries
details.Don't: Skipping the merged dictionary and wondering why Buttons look unstyled
details.Code Bad: <Application.Resources><SolidColorBrush x:Key="MyBrush" Color="Red"/></Application.Resources> with no XamlControlsResources merged

name: uno: Use async [RelayCommand] for I/O
guidance: AsyncRelayCommand reports CanExecute=false (raising CanExecuteChanged) and exposes IsRunning while the Task is in flight — the bound control is disabled and re-entrancy is prevented by default (AllowConcurrentExecutions=false). Do: [RelayCommand] on a Task-returning method for awaitable work. Avoid: async void event handlers calling .Wait() or .Result. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/communitytoolkit/mvvm/generators/relaycommand

details.Description: AsyncRelayCommand reports CanExecute=false (raising CanExecuteChanged) and exposes IsRunning while the Task is in flight — the bound control is disabled and re-entrancy is prevented by default (AllowConcurrentExecutions=false)
details.Do: [RelayCommand] on a Task-returning method for awaitable work
details.Don't: async void event handlers calling .Wait() or .Result
details.Code Good: [RelayCommand] private async Task LoadAsync() { Items = await _api.GetAsync(); }
details.Code Bad: public void OnLoadClick(object s, EventArgs e) { LoadAsync().Wait(); } deadlock risk

name: uno: Use x:Uid for localized strings
guidance: WinUI x:Uid resolves UI text from .resw resources at runtime — use it instead of hardcoded strings to support localization across iOS Android WASM and desktop from a single project. Do: x:Uid on every user-facing string with matching .resw entries per language under Strings/{lang}/Resources.resw. Avoid: Hardcoding language-specific strings into XAML or code-behind. Severity: High. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/working-with-strings.html

details.Description: WinUI x:Uid resolves UI text from .resw resources at runtime — use it instead of hardcoded strings to support localization across iOS Android WASM and desktop from a single project
details.Do: x:Uid on every user-facing string with matching .resw entries per language under Strings/{lang}/Resources.resw
details.Don't: Hardcoding language-specific strings into XAML or code-behind

name: uno: Wire up ILogger via Uno.Extensions.Logging
guidance: Cross-platform logging routes to platform-native sinks (OSLog on iOS Console on WASM Debug elsewhere) when configured through the IHost builder. Do: Inject ILogger<T> into ViewModels and services and call UseLogging() on the host builder. Avoid: Console.WriteLine or platform-specific log APIs scattered across shared code. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/external/uno.extensions/doc/Overview/Logging/LoggingOverview.html

details.Description: Cross-platform logging routes to platform-native sinks (OSLog on iOS Console on WASM Debug elsewhere) when configured through the IHost builder
details.Do: Inject ILogger<T> into ViewModels and services and call UseLogging() on the host builder
details.Don't: Console.WriteLine or platform-specific log APIs scattered across shared code
details.Code Good: Host.CreateDefaultBuilder().UseLogging(c => c.SetMinimumLevel(LogLevel.Information)) and ILogger<MainViewModel> via constructor injection
details.Code Bad: Console.WriteLine("error") in shared code with no platform-aware routing

name: uno: Enable PublishAot on net10.0-desktop
guidance: Skia Desktop on .NET 10 supports Native AOT for faster cold start and smaller deployments — opt in per-target so debug builds remain fast. Do: <PublishAot>true</PublishAot> in a TFM-conditional PropertyGroup for net10.0-desktop release builds. Avoid: Enabling PublishAot globally and breaking debug iteration on every TFM. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/using-skia-desktop.html

details.Description: Skia Desktop on .NET 10 supports Native AOT for faster cold start and smaller deployments — opt in per-target so debug builds remain fast
details.Do: <PublishAot>true</PublishAot> in a TFM-conditional PropertyGroup for net10.0-desktop release builds
details.Don't: Enabling PublishAot globally and breaking debug iteration on every TFM
details.Code Bad: <PublishAot>true</PublishAot> at root with no TFM/Configuration condition

name: uno: Never block on async with .Result or .Wait()
guidance: Blocking on a Task from the UI thread deadlocks because the awaiter cannot resume on the captured SynchronizationContext — always await async APIs through to the event handler. Do: Await async methods all the way up; in libraries call ConfigureAwait(false) to avoid context capture. Avoid: Calling .Result .Wait() or GetAwaiter().GetResult() on a Task from the UI thread. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/asynchronous-programming/

details.Guideline: Never block on async with .Result or .Wait()
details.Description: Blocking on a Task from the UI thread deadlocks because the awaiter cannot resume on the captured SynchronizationContext — always await async APIs through to the event handler
details.Do: Await async methods all the way up; in libraries call ConfigureAwait(false) to avoid context capture
details.Don't: Calling .Result .Wait() or GetAwaiter().GetResult() on a Task from the UI thread
details.Code Good: private async void OnLoadClick(object s, RoutedEventArgs e) { var data = await _api.GetAsync(); Items = data; }
details.Code Bad: private void OnLoadClick(object s, RoutedEventArgs e) { var data = _api.GetAsync().Result; } // deadlocks on UI thread

name: uno: Define ThemeDictionaries for Light Dark and HighContrast
guidance: Resources placed inside ResourceDictionary.ThemeDictionaries entries are automatically swapped when the system theme changes — required for theme-aware brushes. Do: Wrap brushes in a ThemeDictionaries dictionary keyed by Light Dark and HighContrast in App.xaml or page resources. Avoid: Defining a single brush at the root and missing dark/high-contrast variants. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/working-with-themes.html

details.Guideline: Define ThemeDictionaries for Light Dark and HighContrast
details.Description: Resources placed inside ResourceDictionary.ThemeDictionaries entries are automatically swapped when the system theme changes — required for theme-aware brushes
details.Do: Wrap brushes in a ThemeDictionaries dictionary keyed by Light Dark and HighContrast in App.xaml or page resources
details.Don't: Defining a single brush at the root and missing dark/high-contrast variants
details.Code Bad: <SolidColorBrush x:Key="Brand" Color="#005A9E"/> at root with no theme variants

name: uno: Use Uno.Sdk with UnoFeatures
guidance: Uno.Sdk is the modern single-project SDK that auto-resolves Uno.WinUI Uno.Toolkit Material and other packages from a UnoFeatures property — declare features by name instead of hand-managing dozens of PackageReferences. Do: Declare features in the csproj via <UnoFeatures>...</UnoFeatures> and let the SDK resolve transitive packages. Avoid: Hand-adding every Uno.* PackageReference and matching version numbers across packages. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://platform.uno/docs/articles/features/using-the-uno-sdk.html

details.Description: Uno.Sdk is the modern single-project SDK that auto-resolves Uno.WinUI Uno.Toolkit Material and other packages from a UnoFeatures property — declare features by name instead of hand-managing dozens of PackageReferences
details.Do: Declare features in the csproj via <UnoFeatures>...</UnoFeatures> and let the SDK resolve transitive packages
details.Don't: Hand-adding every Uno.* PackageReference and matching version numbers across packages
details.Code Bad: <PackageReference Include="Uno.WinUI"/><PackageReference Include="Uno.Material.WinUI"/> ... duplicated per feature with mismatched versions

name: uno: Persist desktop window state via Window.Closed
guidance: WinAppSDK and Skia desktop heads do not raise Application.Suspending — handle the Window.Closed event (and AppWindow size/position changes) to save user state when desktop apps shut down. Do: Subscribe to MainWindow.Closed and persist any unsaved state before the window is destroyed. Avoid: Relying on Application.Suspending to fire on desktop targets. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/windows-app-sdk/api/winrt/microsoft.ui.xaml.window

details.Guideline: Persist desktop window state via Window.Closed
details.Description: WinAppSDK and Skia desktop heads do not raise Application.Suspending — handle the Window.Closed event (and AppWindow size/position changes) to save user state when desktop apps shut down
details.Do: Subscribe to MainWindow.Closed and persist any unsaved state before the window is destroyed
details.Don't: Relying on Application.Suspending to fire on desktop targets
details.Code Bad: Application.Current.Suspending += SaveState; // never fires on WinAppSDK or Skia desktop

name: uno: Use WinRT.Interop for native window handle on Windows
guidance: Calling Win32 APIs from a WinUI Window (file pickers icon embedding etc.) requires the HWND — retrieve it via WinRT.Interop.WindowNative.GetWindowHandle and guard the call so non-Windows targets stay unaffected. Do: GetWindowHandle inside a #if WINDOWS block when you need the HWND. Avoid: Calling WinRT.Interop in shared code without a platform guard. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/retrieve-hwnd

details.Guideline: Use WinRT.Interop for native window handle on Windows
details.Description: Calling Win32 APIs from a WinUI Window (file pickers icon embedding etc.) requires the HWND — retrieve it via WinRT.Interop.WindowNative.GetWindowHandle and guard the call so non-Windows targets stay unaffected
details.Do: GetWindowHandle inside a #if WINDOWS block when you need the HWND
details.Don't: Calling WinRT.Interop in shared code without a platform guard
details.Code Bad: var hWnd = WinRT.Interop.WindowNative.GetWindowHandle(MainWindow); // breaks build on iOS Android WASM

name: uwp: Use x:Bind for compiled bindings
guidance: Compile-time validated bindings with better performance. Do: x:Bind for type-safe performant bindings. Avoid: {Binding} when x:Bind is available. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/uwp/xaml-platform/x-bind-markup-extension

name: uwp: Use x:Load for deferred elements
guidance: Delay creation of elements until needed. Do: x:Load=False for hidden or conditional panels. Avoid: Loading all UI elements at page load. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/uwp/xaml-platform/x-load-attribute

name: uwp: Use x:Phase for incremental item rendering

name: uwp: Handle back button correctly
guidance: Provide an in-app Back button styled with NavigationBackButtonNormalStyle and handle SystemNavigationManager.BackRequested for hardware back gamepad B and Tablet-Mode back; also handle CoreDispatcher.AcceleratorKeyActivated for Alt+Left. Do: In-app NavigationBackButtonNormalStyle button plus SystemNavigationManager.BackRequested handler. Avoid: Relying on the deprecated title-bar back button (AppViewBackButtonVisibility) or ignoring system back signals. Severity: High. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/uwp/ui-input/back-navigation

details.Description: Provide an in-app Back button styled with NavigationBackButtonNormalStyle and handle SystemNavigationManager.BackRequested for hardware back gamepad B and Tablet-Mode back; also handle CoreDispatcher.AcceleratorKeyActivated for Alt+Left

name: uwp: Use a deferral when saving async state on suspend
guidance: Suspending grants only ~5 seconds before the OS may terminate; await work needs SuspendingOperation.GetDeferral and Complete or save returns before it finishes. Do: GetDeferral around async save calls and Complete in finally. Avoid: Async work that returns the suspending handler before completion. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/uwp/launch-resume/app-lifecycle

details.Guideline: Use a deferral when saving async state on suspend
details.Description: Suspending grants only ~5 seconds before the OS may terminate; await work needs SuspendingOperation.GetDeferral and Complete or save returns before it finishes
details.Do: GetDeferral around async save calls and Complete in finally
details.Don't: Async work that returns the suspending handler before completion
details.Code Good: async void OnSuspending(object s, SuspendingEventArgs e) { var d = e.SuspendingOperation.GetDeferral(); try { await SaveAsync(); } finally { d.Complete(); } }
details.Code Bad: async void OnSuspending(object s, SuspendingEventArgs e) { await SaveAsync(); } // handler returns before save completes

name: winui: Choose Mica vs Acrylic by surface lifetime
guidance: Mica is for long-lived primary surfaces like main windows; Acrylic is for transient light-dismiss surfaces like flyouts and context menus. Do: Mica on root window backgrounds and Acrylic on flyouts and overlays. Avoid: Acrylic on the main window or Mica on transient flyouts. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/design/signature-experiences/materials

details.Guideline: Choose Mica vs Acrylic by surface lifetime
details.Description: Mica is for long-lived primary surfaces like main windows; Acrylic is for transient light-dismiss surfaces like flyouts and context menus

details.Don't: Acrylic on the main window or Mica on transient flyouts
details.Code Bad: Acrylic on every Window background causing battery and perf cost

guidance: WinUI 3 1.3+ exposes Window.SystemBackdrop with MicaBackdrop and DesktopAcrylicBackdrop classes replacing manual MicaController plumbing. Do: Window.SystemBackdrop in XAML or code. Avoid: Hand-rolled MicaController wiring when SystemBackdrop API is available. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/system-backdrops

guidance: WinUI 3 supports multiple top-level windows; each Window owns an AppWindow accessible via Window.AppWindow for size and position control. Do: new Window().Activate() for secondary windows tracking them in App state. Avoid: Faking multi-window via main-window content swaps or ContentDialog. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/ui/manage-app-windows

details.Guideline: Open secondary windows with new Window

guidance: Use Window.ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar with SetTitleBar to host custom XAML in the chrome while preserving caption buttons. guidance: Isolate ViewModel from external dependencies. Do: Moq or NSubstitute for service interfaces. Avoid: Real database calls in unit tests. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/testing/unit-testing-best-practices

details.Description: Isolate ViewModel from external dependencies
details.Do: Moq or NSubstitute for service interfaces

details.Code Good: var mock = new Mock<IDataService>(); var vm = new MainViewModel(mock.Object);

name: wpf: Use UI Automation for integration tests
guidance: Automated UI testing with Microsoft UI Automation. Do: FlaUI or Appium 2 (appium-windows-driver) for end-to-end tests. Avoid: Manual regression testing only. Severity: Medium. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/ui-automation/ui-automation-overview
